This document describes the reconciler's work loop execution and the three phases of processing the Fiber tree: **begin work**, **complete work**, and **commit work**. The work loop is the core mechanism that processes fibers, determines what changed, and applies updates to the host environment. For information about the Fiber data structure itself, see [Fiber Architecture and Data Structures](#4.1). For details on scheduling and priority management, see [Lane-Based Scheduling and Priorities](#4.4). For the hooks system integration, see [React Hooks System](#4.3). ## Overview The work loop is implemented in [packages/react-reconciler/src/ReactFiberWorkLoop.js](). It orchestrates two distinct phases: | Phase | Purpose | Can be interrupted? | |-------|---------|-------------------| | **Render Phase** | Traverse the Fiber tree, compute changes, and mark effects | Yes (in concurrent mode) | | **Commit Phase** | Apply computed changes to the host environment | No (synchronous) | The render phase processes fibers through two sub-phases: `beginWork` and `completeWork`. The commit phase executes through three sub-phases: `commitBeforeMutationEffects`, `commitMutationEffects`, and `commitLayoutEffects`. **Sources:** [packages/react-reconciler/src/ReactFiberWorkLoop.js:1-2000]() ## Work Loop Execution ### Entry Points and Root Processing Work on a root begins through one of two main entry points: ```javascript performSyncWorkOnRoot(root, lanes) // Synchronous, uninterruptible performConcurrentWorkOnRoot(root, lanes) // Concurrent, can yield ``` Both functions follow this pattern: 1. Prepare the work-in-progress root and lanes. 2. Enter the render phase (`renderRootSync` or `renderRootConcurrent`). 3. If render completes successfully, enter commit phase (`commitRoot`). **Render Phase Entry:** ```mermaid graph TB scheduleUpdateOnFiber["scheduleUpdateOnFiber(root, fiber, lane)"] ensureRoot["ensureRootIsScheduled(root)"] performSync["performSyncWorkOnRoot(root, lanes)"] performConcurrent["performConcurrentWorkOnRoot(root, lanes)"] scheduleUpdateOnFiber --> ensureRoot ensureRoot -->|"SyncLane"| performSync ensureRoot -->|"Concurrent Lanes"| performConcurrent performSync --> renderRootSync["renderRootSync(root, lanes)"] performConcurrent --> renderRootConcurrent["renderRootConcurrent(root, lanes)"] renderRootSync --> prepareFresh["prepareFreshStack(root, lanes)
Creates workInProgress tree"] renderRootConcurrent --> prepareFresh prepareFresh --> setContext["executionContext |= RenderContext"] setContext --> workLoopSync["workLoopSync()"] setContext --> workLoopConcurrent["workLoopConcurrent()"] workLoopSync --> exitRender["exitStatus = RootCompleted/RootErrored/etc"] workLoopConcurrent --> exitRender ``` The `prepareFreshStack` function creates the work-in-progress tree by calling `createWorkInProgress(root.current, null)`, which either reuses the existing alternate fiber or creates a new one. **Sources:** [packages/react-reconciler/src/ReactFiberWorkLoop.js:966-1092](), [packages/react-reconciler/src/ReactFiberWorkLoop.js:1116-1300](), [packages/react-reconciler/src/ReactFiber.js:129-234]() ### The Work Loops The actual work loop is the engine that drives the traversal: ```javascript // Synchronous mode - never yields function workLoopSync() { while (workInProgress !== null) { performUnitOfWork(workInProgress); } } // Concurrent mode - yields when shouldYield() returns true function workLoopConcurrent() { while (workInProgress !== null && !shouldYield()) { performUnitOfWork(workInProgress); } } ``` The `workInProgress` variable points to the current Fiber being processed. `performUnitOfWork` processes one unit of work and advances to the next fiber. The `shouldYield()` function is imported from the Scheduler package and checks if the current time slice has expired. In concurrent mode, React will pause work and return control to the browser if `shouldYield()` returns true. **Sources:** [packages/react-reconciler/src/ReactFiberWorkLoop.js:1801-1812](), [packages/react-reconciler/src/Scheduler.js:70-76]() ### Work Loop State Variables The work loop maintains several module-level variables that track the current state: | Variable | Type | Purpose | |----------|------|---------| | `workInProgressRoot` | `FiberRoot \| null` | The root currently being worked on | | `workInProgress` | `Fiber \| null` | The fiber currently being processed | | `workInProgressRootRenderLanes` | `Lanes` | The lanes being rendered | | `workInProgressSuspendedReason` | `SuspendedReason` | Why work is suspended (e.g., SuspendedOnData) | | `workInProgressRootExitStatus` | `RootExitStatus` | Final status: RootCompleted, RootErrored, etc. | | `executionContext` | `ExecutionContext` | Bitmask tracking RenderContext, CommitContext, etc. | **Sources:** [packages/react-reconciler/src/ReactFiberWorkLoop.js:418-458]() ## Render Phase: Begin Work and Complete Work The render phase processes the Fiber tree in a depth-first traversal, calling `beginWork` on the way down and `completeWork` on the way up. ### performUnitOfWork Flow ```mermaid graph TB start["performUnitOfWork(unitOfWork)"] begin["next = beginWork(current, unitOfWork, lanes)"] hasNext{"next !== null?"} complete["completeUnitOfWork(unitOfWork)"] advance["workInProgress = next"] start --> begin begin --> hasNext hasNext -->|"Yes (has child)"| advance hasNext -->|"No (no child)"| complete advance --> return["return to workLoop"] complete --> return ``` **Sources:** [packages/react-reconciler/src/ReactFiberWorkLoop.js:1814-1848]() ### Begin Work Phase The `beginWork` function in [packages/react-reconciler/src/ReactFiberBeginWork.js]() is responsible for creating or updating child fibers based on the fiber's tag. **Begin Work Dispatch by Fiber Tag:** ```mermaid graph TB beginWork["beginWork(current, workInProgress, renderLanes)"] checkBailout{"Can bailout?"} bailout["bailoutOnAlreadyFinishedWork()"] beginWork --> checkBailout checkBailout -->|"Yes"| bailout checkBailout -->|"No"| switch{"workInProgress.tag"} switch -->|"FunctionComponent"| updateFunction["updateFunctionComponent()"] switch -->|"ClassComponent"| updateClass["updateClassComponent()"] switch -->|"HostComponent"| updateHost["updateHostComponent()"] switch -->|"SuspenseComponent"| updateSuspense["updateSuspenseComponent()"] updateFunction --> reconcile["reconcileChildren()"] updateClass --> reconcile updateHost --> reconcile updateSuspense --> reconcile reconcile --> returnChild["return workInProgress.child"] ``` | Function | Responsibility | |----------|----------------| | `updateFunctionComponent` | Calls `renderWithHooks` to execute function body [packages/react-reconciler/src/ReactFiberBeginWork.js:1150-1200]() | | `updateClassComponent` | Manages class lifecycle and state updates [packages/react-reconciler/src/ReactFiberBeginWork.js:1250-1350]() | | `updateHostComponent` | Processes platform-specific elements like `div` [packages/react-reconciler/src/ReactFiberBeginWork.js:1500-1600]() | | `reconcileChildren` | Core diffing algorithm; uses `reconcileChildFibers` or `mountChildFibers` [packages/react-reconciler/src/ReactChildFiber.js:1200-1300]() | **Sources:** [packages/react-reconciler/src/ReactFiberBeginWork.js:3746-4016](), [packages/react-reconciler/src/ReactFiberBeginWork.js:340-371]() ### Complete Work Phase When `beginWork` returns `null`, `completeUnitOfWork` is called. This function: 1. Calls `completeWork` on the current fiber. 2. Collects subtree flags (bubbling up effects). 3. Moves to the next sibling or returns to the parent. **Complete Unit of Work Flow:** ```mermaid graph TB start["completeUnitOfWork(unitOfWork)"] loop["Loop: completedWork = unitOfWork"] complete["completeWork(current, completedWork, lanes)"] bubble["bubbleProperties(completedWork)
Aggregate child flags"] start --> loop loop --> complete complete --> bubble bubble --> hasSibling{"completedWork.sibling !== null?"} hasSibling -->|"Yes"| returnSibling["workInProgress = sibling
Return to performUnitOfWork"] hasSibling -->|"No"| moveParent["completedWork = completedWork.return"] moveParent --> atRoot{"completedWork === null?"} atRoot -->|"Yes (at root)"| exit["workInProgress = null
Render phase complete"] atRoot -->|"No"| loop ``` The `completeWork` function in [packages/react-reconciler/src/ReactFiberCompleteWork.js]() handles: - Creating host instances (e.g., `createInstance` for DOM nodes) [packages/react-reconciler/src/ReactFiberCompleteWork.js:845-900](). - Finalizing props and appending initial children [packages/react-reconciler/src/ReactFiberCompleteWork.js:900-1000](). - Bubbling up `subtreeFlags` so the commit phase knows where work is located [packages/react-reconciler/src/ReactFiberCompleteWork.js:662-724](). **Sources:** [packages/react-reconciler/src/ReactFiberWorkLoop.js:1850-1980](), [packages/react-reconciler/src/ReactFiberCompleteWork.js:845-1400]() ## Commit Phase The commit phase is synchronous and uninterruptible. It applies the changes computed in the render phase to the host environment. ### Commit Sub-Phases The commit phase executes in three sequential sub-phases: #### 1. Before Mutation Phase Run effects that read the DOM before mutations occur (e.g., `getSnapshotBeforeUpdate`). **Key Function:** `commitBeforeMutationEffects` [packages/react-reconciler/src/ReactFiberCommitWork.js:474-569](). #### 2. Mutation Phase Apply all DOM mutations (insertions, updates, deletions). **Key Function:** `commitMutationEffects` [packages/react-reconciler/src/ReactFiberCommitWork.js:1817-1900](). This is where `commitPlacement`, `commitUpdate`, and `commitDeletion` occur [packages/react-reconciler/src/ReactFiberCommitHostEffects.js:1-500](). #### 3. Layout Phase Run effects that need to read the updated DOM layout (e.g., `componentDidMount`, `useLayoutEffect`). **Key Function:** `commitLayoutEffects` [packages/react-reconciler/src/ReactFiberCommitWork.js:590-800](). **Commit Phase Timeline:** ```mermaid graph LR start["Commit Begins"] before["Before Mutation
getSnapshotBeforeUpdate"] mutation["Mutation
Apply DOM changes"] switch["Switch Trees
root.current = finishedWork"] layout["Layout
componentDidMount
useLayoutEffect"] passive["Passive Effects
useEffect (Async)"] start --> before before --> mutation mutation --> switch switch --> layout layout --> passive ``` **Sources:** [packages/react-reconciler/src/ReactFiberWorkLoop.js:2210-2350](), [packages/react-reconciler/src/ReactFiberCommitWork.js:1-1000]() ### Passive Effects (useEffect) Passive effects are scheduled to run after the commit phase completes and the browser has painted. 1. **Scheduling:** `scheduleCallback(NormalPriority, flushPassiveEffects)` is called during commit [packages/react-reconciler/src/ReactFiberWorkLoop.js:2500-2600](). 2. **Execution:** `flushPassiveEffects` runs `commitPassiveUnmountEffects` followed by `commitPassiveMountEffects` [packages/react-reconciler/src/ReactFiberCommitWork.js:2300-2500](). **Sources:** [packages/react-reconciler/src/ReactFiberWorkLoop.js:2500-2900](), [packages/react-reconciler/src/ReactFiberCommitEffects.js:150-300]() ## Error Handling and Suspension If a component suspends or throws an error during the render phase, the work loop catches it and calls `handleThrow`. ```mermaid graph TB beginWork["beginWork throws"] handleThrow["handleThrow(root, thrownValue)"] classify{"SuspendedReason"} beginWork --> handleThrow handleThrow --> classify classify -->|"Data/Promise"| SuspendedOnData["SuspendedOnData"] classify -->|"Error"| SuspendedOnError["SuspendedOnError"] SuspendedOnData --> throwException["throwException(root, returnFiber, thrownValue)"] throwException --> unwind["unwindUnitOfWork(workInProgress)"] ``` `unwindWork` is responsible for popping context stacks and finding the nearest error or suspense boundary [packages/react-reconciler/src/ReactFiberUnwindWork.js:66-200](). **Sources:** [packages/react-reconciler/src/ReactFiberWorkLoop.js:1610-1800](), [packages/react-reconciler/src/ReactFiberThrow.js:200-600](), [packages/react-reconciler/src/ReactFiberUnwindWork.js:1-250]() --- # Page: React Hooks System # React Hooks System

## Purpose and Scope This document describes the React Hooks System implementation, including the dispatcher pattern, hook data structures, effect execution lifecycle, and debugging capabilities. Hooks enable function components to use state and lifecycle features without writing classes. For information about the overall reconciliation process that invokes hooks, see page 4.2 (Work Loop and Rendering Phases) and page 4.1 (Fiber Architecture and Data Structures). For server-side rendering specifics, see page 5.1 (React Fizz Streaming SSR). For static analysis of hook usage, see page 8.3 (ESLint Plugin for React Hooks). ## Architecture Overview The hooks system uses a **dispatcher pattern** to provide different implementations based on component lifecycle phase. During render, hooks resolve to mount, update, or rerender dispatchers. Hook state is stored as a linked list on `fiber.memoizedState`, while effects form a circular list on `fiber.updateQueue.lastEffect`. **Hooks System Architecture** ```mermaid graph TB subgraph "Public API Layer" ReactHooks["React.useState
React.useEffect
React.useCallback
etc."] end subgraph "Dispatcher Resolution" resolveDispatcher["resolveDispatcher()
ReactSharedInternals.H"] MountDispatcher["HooksDispatcherOnMount"] UpdateDispatcher["HooksDispatcherOnUpdate"] RerenderDispatcher["HooksDispatcherOnRerender"] ContextOnlyDispatcher["ContextOnlyDispatcher"] end subgraph "Core Render Function" renderWithHooks["renderWithHooks()
Sets dispatcher, calls component"] finishRenderingHooks["finishRenderingHooks()
Resets state"] end subgraph "Hook Implementation" mountState["mountState()
Initialize hook"] updateState["updateState()
Process updates"] mountEffect["mountEffect()
Create effect"] updateEffect["updateEffect()
Update effect"] end subgraph "Data Structures" HookList["Hook Linked List
fiber.memoizedState"] UpdateQueue["UpdateQueue
pending updates"] EffectList["Effect Circular List
fiber.updateQueue.lastEffect"] end subgraph "Commit Phase" commitHookEffectListMount["commitHookEffectListMount()"] commitHookEffectListUnmount["commitHookEffectListUnmount()"] flushPassiveEffects["flushPassiveEffects()"] end ReactHooks --> resolveDispatcher resolveDispatcher --> MountDispatcher resolveDispatcher --> UpdateDispatcher resolveDispatcher --> RerenderDispatcher resolveDispatcher --> ContextOnlyDispatcher MountDispatcher --> renderWithHooks UpdateDispatcher --> renderWithHooks RerenderDispatcher --> renderWithHooks renderWithHooks --> mountState renderWithHooks --> updateState renderWithHooks --> mountEffect renderWithHooks --> updateEffect renderWithHooks --> finishRenderingHooks mountState --> HookList updateState --> HookList updateState --> UpdateQueue mountEffect --> EffectList updateEffect --> EffectList EffectList --> commitHookEffectListMount EffectList --> commitHookEffectListUnmount EffectList --> flushPassiveEffects ``` Sources: [packages/react-reconciler/src/ReactFiberHooks.js:1-200](), [packages/react/src/ReactHooks.js:1-242]() ## Public Hooks API The public API is exposed through `packages/react/src/ReactHooks.js`. Each hook function calls `resolveDispatcher()` to obtain the current dispatcher from `ReactSharedInternals.H`, then delegates to the dispatcher implementation. **Available Hooks** | Hook | Purpose | Stateful | |------|---------|----------| | `useState` | Local component state | Yes | | `useReducer` | State with reducer logic | Yes | | `useEffect` | Side effects after paint | No | | `useLayoutEffect` | Side effects before paint | No | | `useInsertionEffect` | CSS-in-JS insertions | No | | `useContext` | Read context value | No | | `useRef` | Mutable reference | Yes | | `useMemo` | Memoized value | Yes | | `useCallback` | Memoized callback | Yes | | `useImperativeHandle` | Customize ref value | No | | `useDebugValue` | DevTools label | No | | `useId` | Stable unique ID | Yes | | `useTransition` | Non-blocking updates | Yes | | `useDeferredValue` | Defer value updates | Yes | | `useSyncExternalStore` | Subscribe to external store | Yes | | `useOptimistic` | Optimistic UI updates | Yes | | `useActionState` | Form action state | Yes | | `useEffectEvent` | Stable event handler (experimental) | No | | `useCacheRefresh` | Invalidate cache boundary (experimental) | No | | `use` | Read Promise/Context | No | `useEffectEvent` and `useCacheRefresh` are listed in `HookType` in [packages/react-reconciler/src/ReactInternalTypes.js:46-66]() and exposed through the `Dispatcher` interface at [packages/react-reconciler/src/ReactInternalTypes.js:397-457](). **Dispatcher Resolution Flow** ```mermaid graph LR App["Component calls
useState()"] resolveDispatcher["resolveDispatcher()"] SharedInternals["ReactSharedInternals.H"] CurrentDispatcher{{"Current
Dispatcher?"}} Mount["HooksDispatcherOnMount"] Update["HooksDispatcherOnUpdate"] Rerender["HooksDispatcherOnRerender"] ContextOnly["ContextOnlyDispatcher"] mountState["mountState()"] updateState["updateState()"] App --> resolveDispatcher resolveDispatcher --> SharedInternals SharedInternals --> CurrentDispatcher CurrentDispatcher -->|"First render"| Mount CurrentDispatcher -->|"Subsequent render"| Update CurrentDispatcher -->|"Render phase update"| Rerender CurrentDispatcher -->|"Outside render"| ContextOnly Mount --> mountState Update --> updateState Rerender --> updateState ContextOnly -->|"Throws error"| Error["Invalid hook call"] ``` Sources: [packages/react/src/ReactHooks.js:24-42](), [packages/react/src/ReactHooks.js:66-71](), [packages/react-reconciler/src/ReactInternalTypes.js:397-457]() ## Dispatcher Pattern The dispatcher is a dynamic vtable stored in `ReactSharedInternals.H` that changes based on component lifecycle phase. `renderWithHooks()` sets the appropriate dispatcher before calling the component function, ensuring hooks resolve to the correct implementation. After render, `finishRenderingHooks()` resets the dispatcher to `ContextOnlyDispatcher` to prevent hooks from being called outside of render. **Dispatcher Implementations** | Dispatcher | When Used | Behavior | |------------|-----------|----------| | `HooksDispatcherOnMount` | First render (`current === null` or `memoizedState === null`) | Calls `mountWorkInProgressHook()`, creates linked list | | `HooksDispatcherOnUpdate` | Subsequent renders | Calls `updateWorkInProgressHook()`, processes update queues | | `HooksDispatcherOnRerender` | Render phase updates (`setState` during render) | Used during `renderWithHooksAgain()` re-render loop | | `ContextOnlyDispatcher` | Outside component render | All hooks call `throwInvalidHookError()` | | `HooksDispatcherOnMountInDEV` | DEV first render | Wraps mount dispatcher, records `hookTypesDev` list | | `HooksDispatcherOnUpdateInDEV` | DEV subsequent render | Wraps update dispatcher, validates hook order via `updateHookTypesDev()` | | `HooksDispatcherOnMountWithHookTypesInDEV` | DEV update with no stateful hooks | Edge case: `memoizedState === null` but `hookTypesDev !== null` | | `HooksDispatcherOnRerenderInDEV` | DEV re-render pass | Wraps rerender dispatcher with DEV validation | **renderWithHooks Execution Flow** ```mermaid graph TB Start["renderWithHooks(current, workInProgress, Component)"] SetContext["Set module state:
renderLanes = nextRenderLanes
currentlyRenderingFiber = workInProgress"] ResetFiber["Reset fiber state:
workInProgress.memoizedState = null
workInProgress.updateQueue = null"] CheckCurrent{{"current === null ||
current.memoizedState === null"}} SetMount["ReactSharedInternals.H =
HooksDispatcherOnMount"] SetUpdate["ReactSharedInternals.H =
HooksDispatcherOnUpdate"] CallComponent["children = Component(props, secondArg)"] CheckRerender{{"didScheduleRenderPhaseUpdateDuringThisPass"}} RerenderLoop["renderWithHooksAgain():
Loop while updates scheduled
Use HooksDispatcherOnRerender"] StrictModeCheck{{"StrictMode enabled?"}} DoubleInvoke["Double invoke component
for side effect detection"] Finish["finishRenderingHooks():
Reset dispatcher to ContextOnlyDispatcher"] Return["Return children"] Start --> SetContext SetContext --> ResetFiber ResetFiber --> CheckCurrent CheckCurrent -->|"Yes (mount)"| SetMount CheckCurrent -->|"No (update)"| SetUpdate SetMount --> CallComponent SetUpdate --> CallComponent CallComponent --> CheckRerender CheckRerender -->|"Yes"| RerenderLoop CheckRerender -->|"No"| StrictModeCheck RerenderLoop --> StrictModeCheck StrictModeCheck -->|"Yes"| DoubleInvoke StrictModeCheck -->|"No"| Finish DoubleInvoke --> Finish Finish --> Return ``` Sources: [packages/react-reconciler/src/ReactFiberHooks.js:502-631](), [packages/react-reconciler/src/ReactFiberHooks.js:633-749](), [packages/react-reconciler/src/ReactFiberHooks.js:787-853]() ## Hook Data Structures ### Hook Linked List Each hook call appends a `Hook` object to a singly-linked list stored on `fiber.memoizedState`. The list order must remain constant across renders (enforced by DEV warnings). **Hook Object Structure** ```mermaid graph LR Fiber["Fiber Node"] subgraph "fiber.memoizedState" Hook1["Hook {
memoizedState: any
baseState: any
baseQueue: Update | null
queue: UpdateQueue | null
next: Hook | null
}"] Hook2["Hook {
memoizedState: any
...next
}"] Hook3["Hook {
memoizedState: any
...next
}"] end Fiber --> Hook1 Hook1 -->|"next"| Hook2 Hook2 -->|"next"| Hook3 Hook3 -->|"next"| Null["null"] ``` The `Hook` type is defined in `ReactFiberHooks.js`. Fields: | Field | Type | Purpose | |-------|------|---------| | `memoizedState` | `any` | Current committed state value, or effect object for effect hooks, or `[value, deps]` for `useMemo`/`useCallback` | | `baseState` | `any` | State value before any skipped (lower-priority) updates were applied | | `baseQueue` | `Update \| null` | Circular list of updates that were skipped due to insufficient lane priority | | `queue` | `UpdateQueue \| null` | Pending update queue for `useState`/`useReducer`; `null` for effect/ref hooks | | `next` | `Hook \| null` | Next hook in the linked list | `mountWorkInProgressHook()` creates a new `Hook` and appends it to the list. `updateWorkInProgressHook()` either reuses an existing work-in-progress hook or clones from the `current` fiber's hook list. Sources: [packages/react-reconciler/src/ReactFiberHooks.js:194-200](), [packages/react-reconciler/src/ReactFiberHooks.js:979-998](), [packages/react-reconciler/src/ReactFiberHooks.js:1000-1069]() ### UpdateQueue Structure State hooks (`useState`, `useReducer`) maintain an `UpdateQueue` to track pending updates. Updates form a circular linked list and are processed in order during render. **UpdateQueue and Update Structure** ```mermaid graph TB Hook["Hook"] subgraph "hook.queue (UpdateQueue)" Queue["UpdateQueue {
pending: Update | null
lanes: Lanes
dispatch: Function
lastRenderedReducer: Function
lastRenderedState: any
}"] end subgraph "Circular Update List" Update1["Update {
lane: Lane
action: any
hasEagerState: boolean
eagerState: any
next: Update
}"] Update2["Update {
lane: Lane
action: any
...next
}"] Update3["Update {
lane: Lane
action: any
...next
}"] end Hook -->|"queue"| Queue Queue -->|"pending"| Update3 Update3 -->|"next"| Update1 Update1 -->|"next"| Update2 Update2 -->|"next"| Update3 ``` Sources: [packages/react-reconciler/src/ReactFiberHooks.js:164-181]() ### Effect Circular List Effects (`useEffect`, `useLayoutEffect`, `useInsertionEffect`) are stored in a circular linked list on `fiber.updateQueue.lastEffect`. Each `Effect` object contains the effect function, cleanup function, dependencies, and flags. **Effect and UpdateQueue Data Structures** | Type | Field | Purpose | |------|-------|---------| | `Effect` | `tag: HookFlags` | Bitfield: `HookPassive`, `HookLayout`, `HookInsertion`, `HookHasEffect` | | `Effect` | `inst: EffectInstance` | Shared object `{ destroy: void \| (() => void) }` persisted across renders | | `Effect` | `create` | The user-supplied effect callback | | `Effect` | `deps` | Dependency array (`null` means always run) | | `Effect` | `next` | Next effect in the circular list | | `FunctionComponentUpdateQueue` | `lastEffect` | Tail of the circular effect list (`lastEffect.next` is the head) | | `FunctionComponentUpdateQueue` | `events` | `useEffectEvent` stable refs | | `FunctionComponentUpdateQueue` | `stores` | `useSyncExternalStore` consistency snapshots | | `FunctionComponentUpdateQueue` | `memoCache` | React Compiler's `useMemoCache` storage | The `lastEffect` pointer points to the **last** effect in the circular list, so `lastEffect.next` is the **first** effect. The `EffectInstance` object (`inst`) is shared between the mount and subsequent updates to allow the cleanup function to be stored separately from the effect's other fields. Sources: [packages/react-reconciler/src/ReactFiberHooks.js:202-227](), [packages/react-reconciler/src/ReactFiberHooks.js:246-252]() ## Core Hook Implementations ### State Hooks: useState and useReducer `useState` is implemented as a specialized `useReducer` with a basic state reducer. Both follow the mount/update pattern. **useState/useReducer Implementation Flow** ```mermaid graph TB Call["useState(initialState)
or useReducer(reducer, initialArg, init)"] CreateWIP["createWorkInProgressHook()"] CheckPhase{{"Mount or Update?"}} subgraph "Mount Phase" InitState["Calculate initial state:
- useState: call initializer if function
- useReducer: call init(initialArg) if provided"] CreateQueue["Create UpdateQueue:
queue = {
pending: null,
lanes: NoLanes,
dispatch: null,
lastRenderedReducer: reducer,
lastRenderedState: initialState
}"] BindDispatch["dispatch = dispatchSetState.bind(null, fiber, queue)"] StoreState["hook.memoizedState = initialState
hook.queue = queue"] ReturnMount["Return [initialState, dispatch]"] end subgraph "Update Phase" CloneHook["Clone hook from current:
workInProgressHook = {
memoizedState: currentHook.memoizedState,
baseState: currentHook.baseState,
baseQueue: currentHook.baseQueue,
queue: currentHook.queue
}"] CheckUpdates{{"queue.pending !== null?"}} ProcessQueue["Process update queue:
- Apply baseQueue (skipped updates)
- Apply pending updates
- Calculate new state
- Determine if state changed"] UpdateBaseState["Update baseState, baseQueue
for next render"] ReturnUpdate["Return [newState, dispatch]"] end Call --> CreateWIP CreateWIP --> CheckPhase CheckPhase -->|"Mount"| InitState InitState --> CreateQueue CreateQueue --> BindDispatch BindDispatch --> StoreState StoreState --> ReturnMount CheckPhase -->|"Update"| CloneHook CloneHook --> CheckUpdates CheckUpdates -->|"Yes"| ProcessQueue CheckUpdates -->|"No"| ReturnUpdate ProcessQueue --> UpdateBaseState UpdateBaseState --> ReturnUpdate ``` **Eager State Computation** When `setState` is called, React attempts to compute the new state eagerly (outside render) if the queue is empty. If the new state equals the current state (using `Object.is`), React can bail out of the render entirely. ```mermaid graph TB SetState["setState(action)
or dispatch(action)"] CheckContext{{"Is inside render?"}} RenderPhaseUpdate["Enqueue render phase update:
Store in renderPhaseUpdates Map"] CreateUpdate["Create Update object:
update = {
lane: requestUpdateLane(),
action: action,
hasEagerState: false,
eagerState: null,
next: null
}"] EnqueueConcurrent["enqueueConcurrentHookUpdate(fiber, queue, update, lane)"] CheckEager{{"Queue is empty &
alternate exists?"}} ComputeEager["Eager computation:
eagerState = lastRenderedReducer(lastRenderedState, action)"] CheckSame{{"Object.is(eagerState, currentState)?"}} MarkEager["update.hasEagerState = true
update.eagerState = eagerState"] BailOut["Bail out:
Do not schedule update
Return immediately"] ScheduleUpdate["scheduleUpdateOnFiber(fiber, lane)"] SetState --> CheckContext CheckContext -->|"Yes"| RenderPhaseUpdate CheckContext -->|"No"| CreateUpdate CreateUpdate --> EnqueueConcurrent EnqueueConcurrent --> CheckEager CheckEager -->|"Yes"| ComputeEager CheckEager -->|"No"| ScheduleUpdate ComputeEager --> CheckSame CheckSame -->|"Yes"| MarkEager CheckSame -->|"No"| ScheduleUpdate MarkEager --> BailOut ``` The `dispatchSetState` function (for `useState`) and `dispatchReducerAction` function (for `useReducer`) handle all state updates triggered outside of render. Sources: [packages/react-reconciler/src/ReactFiberHooks.js:1094-1250]() ### Effect Hooks: useEffect, useLayoutEffect, useInsertionEffect Effect hooks create `Effect` objects and append them to the circular effect list. The `tag` field determines when the effect is executed: - `HookInsertion`: During commit before mutations (for CSS-in-JS) - `HookLayout`: During commit after mutations, before paint (sync) - `HookPassive`: After commit and paint (async via scheduler) **Effect Mount and Update** ```mermaid graph TB CallEffect["useEffect(create, deps)"] CreateWIP["createWorkInProgressHook()"] CheckPhase{{"Mount or Update?"}} subgraph "Mount Phase" CreateEffect["Create Effect object:
effect = {
tag: HookPassive | HookHasEffect,
inst: { destroy: undefined },
create: create,
deps: deps,
next: null
}"] AppendEffect["Append to circular list:
fiber.updateQueue.lastEffect = effect"] MarkFlag["Mark fiber flag:
fiber.flags |= PassiveEffect"] end subgraph "Update Phase" CheckDeps{{"areHookInputsEqual(nextDeps, prevDeps)?"}} CreateNoOpEffect["Create Effect without HookHasEffect:
effect.tag = HookPassive
(won't fire)"] CreateNewEffect["Create Effect with HookHasEffect:
effect.tag = HookPassive | HookHasEffect
(will fire)"] UpdateList["Update circular list"] end CallEffect --> CreateWIP CreateWIP --> CheckPhase CheckPhase -->|"Mount"| CreateEffect CreateEffect --> AppendEffect AppendEffect --> MarkFlag CheckPhase -->|"Update"| CheckDeps CheckDeps -->|"Deps unchanged"| CreateNoOpEffect CheckDeps -->|"Deps changed"| CreateNewEffect CreateNoOpEffect --> UpdateList CreateNewEffect --> UpdateList UpdateList --> MarkFlag ``` The `HookHasEffect` flag is the key signal: effects only run during commit if `(effect.tag & HookHasEffect) !== 0`. On mount all effects have it set; on update only effects with changed deps get it. Sources: [packages/react-reconciler/src/ReactFiberHooks.js:1955-2070]() ### Ref Hook: useRef `useRef` is a simple hook that stores a mutable object with a `current` property. The ref object identity remains stable across renders. ```mermaid graph LR Call["useRef(initialValue)"] CreateWIP["createWorkInProgressHook()"] CheckPhase{{"Mount or Update?"}} Mount["Mount:
ref = { current: initialValue }
hook.memoizedState = ref"] Update["Update:
ref = hook.memoizedState"] Return["Return ref"] Call --> CreateWIP CreateWIP --> CheckPhase CheckPhase -->|"Mount"| Mount CheckPhase -->|"Update"| Update Mount --> Return Update --> Return ``` Sources: [packages/react-reconciler/src/ReactFiberHooks.js:1762-1779]() ### Memoization Hooks: useMemo and useCallback `useMemo` and `useCallback` cache computed values based on dependency arrays. During mount, they compute and store the value. During update, they check dependencies and either reuse or recompute. **useMemo Implementation** ```mermaid graph TB Call["useMemo(create, deps)"] CreateWIP["createWorkInProgressHook()"] CheckPhase{{"Mount or Update?"}} subgraph "Mount Phase" CallCreate["value = create()"] StoreMount["hook.memoizedState = [value, deps]"] ReturnMount["Return value"] end subgraph "Update Phase" GetPrev["prevState = hook.memoizedState
[prevValue, prevDeps] = prevState"] CheckDeps{{"areHookInputsEqual(nextDeps, prevDeps)?"}} ReuseValue["Return prevValue"] RecomputeValue["value = create()
hook.memoizedState = [value, nextDeps]"] ReturnNew["Return value"] end Call --> CreateWIP CreateWIP --> CheckPhase CheckPhase -->|"Mount"| CallCreate CallCreate --> StoreMount StoreMount --> ReturnMount CheckPhase -->|"Update"| GetPrev GetPrev --> CheckDeps CheckDeps -->|"Yes"| ReuseValue CheckDeps -->|"No"| RecomputeValue RecomputeValue --> ReturnNew ``` `useCallback(fn, deps)` stores `[fn, deps]` in `memoizedState` and returns the cached `fn` when deps are unchanged, avoiding the overhead of calling a factory function. Sources: [packages/react-reconciler/src/ReactFiberHooks.js:1888-1955]() ## Effect Execution Lifecycle Effects are executed during the commit phase, but at different times based on their type. The fiber flags (`PassiveEffect`, `UpdateEffect`) indicate which effects need to be processed. **Effect Execution Timeline** ```mermaid graph TB subgraph "Render Phase" RenderComponent["renderWithHooks()
Component executes"] CollectEffects["Effects collected in
fiber.updateQueue.lastEffect"] MarkFlags["Set fiber.flags:
PassiveEffect, UpdateEffect, etc."] end subgraph "Commit Phase - Before Mutation" BeforeMutation["commitBeforeMutationEffects()"] Snapshots["No effect execution
(getSnapshotBeforeUpdate)"] end subgraph "Commit Phase - Mutation" Mutation["commitMutationEffects()"] InsertionUnmount["commitHookEffectListUnmount(HookInsertion):
Run insertion effect cleanups"] end subgraph "Commit Phase - Layout" Layout["commitLayoutEffects()"] InsertionMount["commitHookEffectListMount(HookInsertion):
Run insertion effects"] LayoutUnmount["commitHookEffectListUnmount(HookLayout):
Run layout effect cleanups"] LayoutMount["commitHookEffectListMount(HookLayout):
Run layout effects (useLayoutEffect)"] end subgraph "After Paint - Async" SchedulePassive["scheduleCallback(NormalPriority, flushPassiveEffects)"] FlushPassive["flushPassiveEffects()"] PassiveUnmount["commitHookPassiveUnmountEffects():
Run passive effect cleanups"] PassiveMount["commitHookPassiveMountEffects():
Run passive effects (useEffect)"] end RenderComponent --> CollectEffects CollectEffects --> MarkFlags MarkFlags --> BeforeMutation BeforeMutation --> Snapshots Snapshots --> Mutation Mutation --> InsertionUnmount InsertionUnmount --> Layout Layout --> InsertionMount InsertionMount --> LayoutUnmount LayoutUnmount --> LayoutMount LayoutMount --> SchedulePassive SchedulePassive --> FlushPassive FlushPassive --> PassiveUnmount PassiveUnmount --> PassiveMount ``` **Effect Tag Bits** | Tag | Constant | Meaning | |-----|----------|---------| | `HookHasEffect` | `0b001` | Effect should fire this render | | `HookInsertion` | `0b010` | Insertion effect (CSS-in-JS) | | `HookLayout` | `0b100` | Layout effect (sync after mutations) | | `HookPassive` | `0b1000` | Passive effect (async after paint) | Effects only execute if `effect.tag & HookHasEffect !== 0`. During mount, all effects have `HookHasEffect`. During update, only effects with changed dependencies have `HookHasEffect`. Sources: [packages/react-reconciler/src/ReactHookEffectTags.js:1-21](), [packages/react-reconciler/src/ReactFiberHooks.js:94-99]() ## Server-Side Hooks Implementation The Fizz server renderer (`packages/react-server/src/ReactFizzHooks.js`) provides a separate hooks implementation optimized for synchronous server rendering. Key differences: - **No effects**: `useEffect`, `useLayoutEffect`, `useInsertionEffect` are no-ops - **Simpler state management**: No concurrent updates or lanes - **Synchronous execution**: No scheduling or yielding - **Render phase updates**: Handled via re-render loop with `renderPhaseUpdates` Map **Server Hook State Management** ```mermaid graph TB PrepareHooks["prepareToUseHooks(request, task, componentIdentity)"] SetContext["Set module state:
currentlyRenderingComponent = componentIdentity
currentlyRenderingTask = task
currentlyRenderingRequest = request"] CallComponent["Component(props, context)"] CreateWIPHook["createWorkInProgressHook():
Create or reuse hook"] CheckRerender{{"didScheduleRenderPhaseUpdate?"}} RerenderLoop["Loop while updates scheduled:
Apply renderPhaseUpdates
Re-call Component()"] FinishHooks["finishHooks():
Return children"] ResetState["resetHooksState():
Clear module state"] PrepareHooks --> SetContext SetContext --> CallComponent CallComponent --> CreateWIPHook CreateWIPHook --> CheckRerender CheckRerender -->|"Yes"| RerenderLoop RerenderLoop --> CheckRerender CheckRerender -->|"No"| FinishHooks FinishHooks --> ResetState ``` **Server `useActionState` Hook** `useActionState` (formerly `useFormState`) is special on the server. It receives permalink information and action state from the request, enabling progressive enhancement of forms. The server implementation uses `actionStateCounter` and `actionStateMatchingIndex` module-level counters (set in `prepareToUseHooks`) to match the correct form state during multi-page navigation, which are read back through `getActionStateCount()` and `getActionStateMatchingIndex()`. Sources: [packages/react-server/src/ReactFizzHooks.js:206-298](), [packages/react-server/src/ReactFizzHooks.js:665-831]() ## Debug and DevTools Integration ### ReactDebugHooks The `react-debug-tools` package provides `inspectHooks()` and `inspectHooksOfFiber()` to extract hook information for DevTools. It uses a custom dispatcher that logs all hook calls. **Hook Inspection Architecture** ```mermaid graph TB DevTools["React DevTools"] InspectAPI["ReactDebugTools.inspectHooksOfFiber(fiber)"] subgraph "Inspection Process" SaveDispatcher["Save current dispatcher"] SetDebugDispatcher["Set ReactSharedInternals.H = DispatcherProxy"] SetupContext["Setup currentFiber, currentHook
from fiber.memoizedState"] ReplayRender["Call Component(props)
to replay render"] LogHooks["Dispatcher logs each hook call:
hookLog.push({ primitive, value, stackError })"] RestoreDispatcher["Restore original dispatcher"] BuildTree["buildTree(rootStack, hookLog):
Parse stack traces
Construct hook tree with nesting"] end ReturnTree["Return HooksTree:
Array of HooksNode"] DevTools --> InspectAPI InspectAPI --> SaveDispatcher SaveDispatcher --> SetDebugDispatcher SetDebugDispatcher --> SetupContext SetupContext --> ReplayRender ReplayRender --> LogHooks LogHooks --> RestoreDispatcher RestoreDispatcher --> BuildTree BuildTree --> ReturnTree ReturnTree --> DevTools ``` **HooksNode Structure** ```typescript type HooksNode = { id: number | null, // Hook index for state hooks isStateEditable: boolean, // Can DevTools edit this state? name: string, // Hook name (e.g., "State", "Effect", "Custom") value: mixed, // Hook value subHooks: Array, // Nested custom hook calls debugInfo: ReactDebugInfo | null, // Debug metadata hookSource: HookSource | null // Source location { fileName, lineNumber, etc. } } ``` The debug dispatcher intercepts every hook call, extracts its value from the fiber's hook list, and records it in `hookLog`. Stack trace parsing determines which hooks are nested inside custom hooks. Sources: [packages/react-debug-tools/src/ReactDebugHooks.js:1-300](), [packages/react-debug-tools/src/ReactDebugHooks.js:996-1098]() ### DEV-Only Validation In development builds, React enforces the Rules of Hooks through several mechanisms: **Hook Order Validation** ```mermaid graph TB Render["Component renders"] CheckDev{{"__DEV__ mode?"}} SaveTypes["finishRenderingHooks():\nworkInProgress._debugHookTypes = hookTypesDev"] MountHook["mountHookTypesDev():\nhookTypesDev.push(currentHookNameInDev)"] UpdateHook["updateHookTypesDev():\nhookTypesUpdateIndexDev++\ncheck hookTypesDev[index]"] Mismatch{{"Hook type mismatch?"}} WarnMismatch["warnOnHookMismatchInDev():\nconsole.error with table\nof previous vs current hooks"] CheckDeps["checkDepsAreArrayDev(deps):\nwarn if deps is not an array"] CheckDepLength["areHookInputsEqual():\nwarn if deps.length changed"] Continue["Continue render"] Render --> CheckDev CheckDev -->|"Yes"| SaveTypes CheckDev -->|"No"| Continue SaveTypes --> MountHook SaveTypes --> UpdateHook UpdateHook --> Mismatch Mismatch -->|"Yes"| WarnMismatch Mismatch -->|"No"| CheckDeps WarnMismatch --> Continue CheckDeps --> CheckDepLength CheckDepLength --> Continue ``` **Validated Conditions** | Rule | Function | Error / Warning | |------|----------|-----------------| | Hook call order | `updateHookTypesDev()` via `warnOnHookMismatchInDev()` | "React has detected a change in the order of Hooks" | | Dependency array type | `checkDepsAreArrayDev(deps)` | "received a final argument that is not an array" | | Dependency array length | `areHookInputsEqual()` | "changed size between renders" | | Effect callback present | `useEffect` / `useLayoutEffect` / `useInsertionEffect` wrappers in `ReactHooks.js` | "requires an effect callback" | | Valid render context | `throwInvalidHookError()` via `ContextOnlyDispatcher` | "Invalid hook call. Hooks can only be called inside of the body of a function component." | | No async client components | `warnIfAsyncClientComponent()` | "is an async Client Component" | | `use()` not in try/catch | `checkIfUseWrappedInTryCatch()` in `finishRenderingHooks()` | "`use` was called from inside a try/catch block" | Sources: [packages/react-reconciler/src/ReactFiberHooks.js:308-394](), [packages/react-reconciler/src/ReactFiberHooks.js:410-451](), [packages/react-reconciler/src/ReactFiberHooks.js:729-748]() ## Hook Lifecycle Summary **Complete Hook Execution Flow** ```mermaid graph TB subgraph "Initial Mount" Mount1["renderWithHooks() sets HooksDispatcherOnMount"] Mount2["Component calls hooks"] Mount3["mountWorkInProgressHook() creates Hook nodes"] Mount4["Hooks stored in fiber.memoizedState linked list"] Mount5["Effects stored in fiber.updateQueue.lastEffect"] Mount6["Fiber marked with PassiveEffect, UpdateEffect flags"] end subgraph "Update Render" Update1["renderWithHooks() sets HooksDispatcherOnUpdate"] Update2["Component calls hooks"] Update3["updateWorkInProgressHook() clones from current"] Update4["Process update queues"] Update5["Compare dependencies for effects"] Update6["Mark changed effects with HookHasEffect"] end subgraph "Commit Effects" Commit1["Commit phase begins"] Commit2["commitMutationEffects(): Insertion effect cleanups"] Commit3["commitLayoutEffects(): Insertion + Layout effects"] Commit4["Layout effect cleanups, then mounts"] Commit5["scheduleCallback for passive effects"] Commit6["After paint: flushPassiveEffects()"] Commit7["Passive effect cleanups, then mounts"] end subgraph "setState Called" SetState1["setState(action) during event"] SetState2["Create Update object"] SetState3["Eager state computation if possible"] SetState4["enqueueConcurrentHookUpdate()"] SetState5["scheduleUpdateOnFiber()"] SetState6["Triggers new render"] end Mount1 --> Mount2 Mount2 --> Mount3 Mount3 --> Mount4 Mount4 --> Mount5 Mount5 --> Mount6 Mount6 --> Commit1 Update1 --> Update2 Update2 --> Update3 Update3 --> Update4 Update4 --> Update5 Update5 --> Update6 Update6 --> Commit1 Commit1 --> Commit2 Commit2 --> Commit3 Commit3 --> Commit4 Commit4 --> Commit5 Commit5 --> Commit6 Commit6 --> Commit7 SetState1 --> SetState2 SetState2 --> SetState3 SetState3 --> SetState4 SetState4 --> SetState5 SetState5 --> SetState6 SetState6 --> Update1 ``` Sources: [packages/react-reconciler/src/ReactFiberHooks.js:502-977]() ## ## Purpose and Scope This page describes the lane model that React's reconciler uses to prioritize, schedule, and track concurrent work. It covers the lane bit-field representation, how lanes are assigned to updates, how the root tracks pending work, how the work loop selects which lanes to process, entanglement, expiration, and the bridge between lanes and the underlying scheduler. For the broader work loop that consumes lanes, see [Work Loop and Rendering Phases](4.2). For how Suspense interacts with retry lanes, see [Suspense, Error Boundaries, and Offscreen](4.5). For the underlying scheduler package that lanes map to, see [React Reconciler](4). --- ## Lane Model Overview A **lane** is a 32-bit integer where each bit position represents a distinct priority channel. The `Lanes` type is a bitmask of one or more lanes. This design lets the reconciler: - Represent multiple simultaneous priorities as bitwise OR combinations. - Test membership with bitwise AND (`includesSomeLane`, `isSubsetOfLanes`). - Merge and remove sets efficiently (`mergeLanes`, `removeLanes`). - Pick the highest-priority lane by finding the lowest set bit. There are **31 usable lane bits** (the 32nd is unused). The constant `TotalLanes = 31` is defined in `ReactFiberLane.js` [packages/react-reconciler/src/ReactFiberLane.js:127]() and consumed by `ReactFiberReconciler.js` [packages/react-reconciler/src/ReactFiberReconciler.js:127](). The central file is: - **`packages/react-reconciler/src/ReactFiberLane.js`** — defines all lane constants, lane-map types, and the operations on them. Sources: [packages/react-reconciler/src/ReactFiberWorkLoop.js:179-221](), [packages/react-reconciler/src/ReactFiberReconciler.js:95-101](), [packages/react-reconciler/src/ReactFiberBeginWork.js:153-166]() --- ## Lane Constants and Priority Groups Lanes are ordered from highest priority (lowest bit) to lowest priority (highest bit). The table below lists the named lane constants exported by `ReactFiberLane.js` and the performance track group each belongs to (as determined by `getGroupNameOfHighestPriorityLane` [packages/react-reconciler/src/ReactFiberLane.js:23](), which is used in `ReactFiberPerformanceTrack.js` [packages/react-reconciler/src/ReactFiberPerformanceTrack.js:58]()). | Lane Constant | Priority Group | Typical Trigger | |---|---|---| | `SyncLane` | Blocking | `flushSync`, legacy roots, error recovery | | `InputContinuousHydrationLane` | Blocking | Hydration at continuous input priority | | `InputContinuousLane` | Blocking | Continuous input events (pointer drag, etc.) | | `DefaultHydrationLane` | Blocking | Hydration at default priority | | `DefaultLane` | Blocking | `setState`, `root.render()`, discrete events | | `SyncUpdateLanes` | Blocking | Combined sync-equivalent lanes | | `GestureLane` | Gesture | Gesture-driven view transitions | | `TransitionLane1`–`TransitionLane16` | Transition | `startTransition` updates | | `RetryLane1`–`RetryLane4` | Suspense | Suspense retry after promise resolves | | `SelectiveHydrationLane` | — | User-event-triggered selective hydration | | `IdleHydrationLane` | Idle | Background SSR hydration | | `IdleLane` | Idle | Idle-priority background work | | `OffscreenLane` | — | Deferred / hidden-subtree pre-rendering | Convenience sentinel values: | Constant | Meaning | |---|---| | `NoLane` | Zero — no lane | | `NoLanes` | Zero — empty set | | `SomeRetryLane` | A non-specific retry lane used for comparisons | | `SomeTransitionLane` | A non-specific transition lane for comparisons | | `UpdateLanes` | Union of all non-idle, non-offscreen update lanes | Sources: [packages/react-reconciler/src/ReactFiberWorkLoop.js:179-221](), [packages/react-reconciler/src/ReactFiberPerformanceTrack.js:22-110](), [packages/react-reconciler/src/ReactFiberLane.js:180-221]() --- **Lane Hierarchy and Code Entities** ```mermaid graph TB subgraph "ReactFiberLane.js Constants (highest → lowest priority)" SL["SyncLane"] ICL["InputContinuousLane"] DL["DefaultLane"] GL["GestureLane"] TL["TransitionLane1..16"] RL["RetryLane1..4"] SHL["SelectiveHydrationLane"] IL["IdleLane"] OL["OffscreenLane"] end SL --> ICL --> DL --> GL --> TL --> RL --> SHL --> IL --> OL subgraph "Scheduler Priority (Scheduler.js)" ImmP["ImmediatePriority"] UserP["UserBlockingPriority"] NormP["NormalPriority"] IdleP["IdlePriority"] end SL -- "eventPriorityToLane" --> ImmP ICL -- "lanesToEventPriority" --> UserP DL -- "lanesToEventPriority" --> NormP TL -- "lanesToEventPriority" --> NormP IL -- "lanesToEventPriority" --> IdleP subgraph "EventPriority bridge (ReactEventPriorities.js)" DEP["DiscreteEventPriority"] ICEP["InputContinuousEventPriority (DefaultEventPriority)"] end ImmP --- DEP NormP --- ICEP ``` Sources: [packages/react-reconciler/src/ReactFiberWorkLoop.js:222-228](), [packages/react-reconciler/src/ReactEventPriorities.js:223-228]() --- ## FiberRoot Lane Tracking Fields Every `FiberRoot` object carries a set of lane bitmasks that record the state of pending work. These fields are read by `getNextLanes` [packages/react-reconciler/src/ReactFiberLane.js:197]() to decide what to render next. | Field | Type | Role | |---|---|---| | `pendingLanes` | `Lanes` | All lanes with unconsumed work | | `suspendedLanes` | `Lanes` | Lanes that are suspended (awaiting a ping) | | `pingedLanes` | `Lanes` | Suspended lanes that have been pinged (resolved) | | `expiredLanes` | `Lanes` | Lanes whose deadline has passed; must run synchronously | | `finishedLanes` | `Lanes` | Lanes included in the most recent completed render | | `errorRecoveryDisabledLanes` | `Lanes` | Lanes where error recovery has been suppressed | | `entangledLanes` | `Lanes` | Lanes that have entanglement dependencies | | `entanglements` | `LaneMap` | Per-lane sets of entangled lanes that must commit together | | `expirationTimes` | `LaneMap` | Wall-clock deadline for each lane | The functions that mutate these fields are defined in `ReactFiberLane.js` and imported into `ReactFiberWorkLoop.js`: - `markRootUpdated(root, lane)` [packages/react-reconciler/src/ReactFiberLane.js:202]() — sets bits in `pendingLanes`. - `markRootSuspended(root, lanes)` [packages/react-reconciler/src/ReactFiberLane.js:201]() — moves lanes from `pending` to `suspended`. - `markRootPinged(root, pingedLanes)` [packages/react-reconciler/src/ReactFiberLane.js:203]() — marks `pingedLanes` bits so they can retry. - `markRootFinished(root, finishedLanes)` [packages/react-reconciler/src/ReactFiberLane.js:204]() — clears the finished lanes. Sources: [packages/react-reconciler/src/ReactFiberWorkLoop.js:200-207]() --- **FiberRoot Lane State Machine** ```mermaid stateDiagram-v2 [*] --> "pendingLanes" "pendingLanes" --> "suspendedLanes" : "markRootSuspended()" "suspendedLanes" --> "pingedLanes" : "markRootPinged() (promise resolved)" "pingedLanes" --> "pendingLanes" : "getNextLanes() re-includes pinged" "pendingLanes" --> "expiredLanes" : "expiration deadline exceeded" "expiredLanes" --> "pendingLanes" : "upgradePendingLanesToSync()" "pendingLanes" --> [*] : "markRootFinished() clears lane" ``` Sources: [packages/react-reconciler/src/ReactFiberWorkLoop.js:200-207](), [packages/react-reconciler/src/ReactFiberWorkLoop.js:196-199]() --- ## Assigning a Lane to an Update: `requestUpdateLane` `requestUpdateLane(fiber)` is called at the top of every state update path — `setState`, `dispatchAction`, `updateContainer`, etc. — to determine which lane the new update belongs to. Defined at [packages/react-reconciler/src/ReactFiberWorkLoop.js:804-848] (note: specific lines may vary in full file). **Decision logic:** 1. **Legacy mode root** (`ConcurrentMode` not set on the fiber) → always returns `SyncLane`. 2. **Render-phase update** (`executionContext & RenderContext` is set) → returns `pickArbitraryLane(workInProgressRootRenderLanes)` [packages/react-reconciler/src/ReactFiberLane.js:188](). 3. **Inside `startTransition`** (`requestCurrentTransition()` [packages/react-reconciler/src/ReactFiberTransition.js:229]() returns non-null) → claims a transition lane. 4. **Normal event-driven update** → calls `eventPriorityToLane(resolveUpdatePriority())` [packages/react-reconciler/src/ReactFiberConfig.js:119](). ```mermaid flowchart TD A["requestUpdateLane(fiber)"] B{"Legacy mode?"} C["return SyncLane"] D{"Inside render phase?"} E["return pickArbitraryLane(workInProgressRootRenderLanes)"] F{"Inside startTransition?"} G["return requestTransitionLane(transition)"] H["resolveUpdatePriority() from host config"] I["eventPriorityToLane()"] J["return lane"] A --> B B -- "yes" --> C B -- "no" --> D D -- "yes" --> E D -- "no" --> F F -- "yes" --> G F -- "no" --> H --> I --> J ``` Sources: [packages/react-reconciler/src/ReactFiberWorkLoop.js:804-848](), [packages/react-reconciler/src/ReactFiberLane.js:182-221]() --- ## Scheduling Work: `ensureRootIsScheduled` After an update is enqueued and `markRootUpdated` records the lane on the root, `scheduleUpdateOnFiber` [packages/react-reconciler/src/ReactFiberWorkLoop.js:71]() eventually ensures the root is scheduled. `ensureRootIsScheduled(root)` determines the next priority to schedule: - If the result includes `SyncLane` [packages/react-reconciler/src/ReactFiberLane.js:182]() or expired lanes, it schedules a synchronous flush. - Otherwise, it schedules a Scheduler callback via `Scheduler_scheduleCallback` [packages/react-reconciler/src/ReactFiberWorkLoop.js:71](). The mapping from lanes to Scheduler priorities goes through `lanesToEventPriority` [packages/react-reconciler/src/ReactEventPriorities.js:226](). Sources: [packages/react-reconciler/src/ReactFiberWorkLoop.js:69-77](), [packages/react-reconciler/src/ReactFiberWorkLoop.js:403-406]() --- ## Selecting Lanes to Render: `getNextLanes` `getNextLanes(root, wipLanes)` [packages/react-reconciler/src/ReactFiberLane.js:197]() is called at the start of every render attempt. 1. If `pendingLanes === NoLanes`, return `NoLanes`. 2. **Expired lanes** (`expiredLanes`) [packages/react-reconciler/src/ReactFiberLane.js:196]() are always included. 3. **Entangled lanes** [packages/react-reconciler/src/ReactFiberLane.js:198]() are merged in to ensure consistent commits. The result is stored in `workInProgressRootRenderLanes` at the start of a render. Sources: [packages/react-reconciler/src/ReactFiberWorkLoop.js:196-221](), [packages/react-reconciler/src/ReactFiberLane.js:197-198]() --- ## Lanes During Rendering Every fiber is processed with `renderLanes` threaded through `beginWork` [packages/react-reconciler/src/ReactFiberBeginWork.js:232](). During `beginWork`, React checks if a fiber has pending updates in the current render. If the fiber's `lanes` field is not a subset of `renderLanes` (checked via `isSubsetOfLanes` [packages/react-reconciler/src/ReactFiberLane.js:185]()), the update is skipped. In `processUpdateQueue` [packages/react-reconciler/src/ReactFiberClassUpdateQueue.js:147](), each update's `lane` is compared against the `renderLanes`. Sources: [packages/react-reconciler/src/ReactFiberBeginWork.js:153-166](), [packages/react-reconciler/src/ReactFiberClassUpdateQueue.js:147-152]() --- ## Lane Entanglement Entanglement couples lanes so they must commit together. This is crucial for consistency in `startTransition`. - `entangleTransitions(root, fiber, lane)` [packages/react-reconciler/src/ReactFiberClassUpdateQueue.js:86]() sets bits in `root.entangledLanes`. - `getEntangledLanes(root, renderLanes)` [packages/react-reconciler/src/ReactFiberLane.js:198]() expands the selected lane set. Sources: [packages/react-reconciler/src/ReactFiberLane.js:196-221](), [packages/react-reconciler/src/ReactFiberClassUpdateQueue.js:86-87]() --- ## Retry Lanes Retry lanes are used by Suspense. When a suspended promise resolves, React calls `claimNextRetryLane()` [packages/react-reconciler/src/ReactFiberLane.js:183]() to get a lane for the retry render. - `SomeRetryLane` [packages/react-reconciler/src/ReactFiberLane.js:219]() is a mask for all retry lanes. - `includesRetryLane(lanes)` [packages/react-reconciler/src/ReactFiberLane.js:194]() checks if any retry lane is present. Sources: [packages/react-reconciler/src/ReactFiberWorkLoop.js:850-862](), [packages/react-reconciler/src/ReactFiberLane.js:183-219]() --- ## Offscreen and Deferred Lanes ### `OffscreenLane` `OffscreenLane` [packages/react-reconciler/src/ReactFiberLane.js:210]() is assigned to hidden subtrees. When a fiber is hidden, its work is deferred to this lane. ### `requestDeferredLane` Used by `useDeferredValue`. It claims a transition lane via `claimNextTransitionDeferredLane()` [packages/react-reconciler/src/ReactFiberLane.js:213]() to ensure the deferred value update doesn't block the primary render. Sources: [packages/react-reconciler/src/ReactFiberWorkLoop.js:864-901](), [packages/react-reconciler/src/ReactFiberLane.js:210-213]() --- ## Summary: Lane Lifecycle ```mermaid sequenceDiagram participant UC as "User Code (setState)" participant RUL as "requestUpdateLane()" participant EQ as "enqueueUpdate()" participant MRU as "markRootUpdated()" participant ERS as "ensureRootIsScheduled()" participant GNL as "getNextLanes()" participant WL as "Work Loop (renderRootConcurrent)" participant CR as "commitRoot()" participant MRF as "markRootFinished()" UC ->> RUL: fiber RUL -->> UC: lane UC ->> EQ: update + lane EQ ->> MRU: root, lane MRU ->> ERS: root ERS ->> GNL: root GNL -->> ERS: renderLanes ERS ->> WL: schedule callback WL ->> WL: workInProgressRootRenderLanes = renderLanes WL ->> CR: finishedWork, lanes CR ->> MRF: root, finishedLanes MRF ->> MRF: pendingLanes &= ~finishedLanes ``` Sources: [packages/react-reconciler/src/ReactFiberWorkLoop.js:804-848](), [packages/react-reconciler/src/ReactFiberLane.js:200-221](), [packages/react-reconciler/src/ReactFiberWorkLoop.js:403-406]() --- # Page: Suspense, Error Boundaries, and Offscreen # Suspense, Error Boundaries, and Offscreen

## Purpose and Scope This document describes how React handles asynchronous operations, errors, and deferred rendering through three key mechanisms: **Suspense boundaries**, **Error boundaries**, and the **Offscreen** (or **Activity**) component. Suspense boundaries enable components to declaratively wait for asynchronous data by catching thrown promises and rendering fallback UI. Error boundaries catch JavaScript errors during the render phase to prevent application crashes. The Offscreen component provides a mechanism for "hiding" and "prerendering" trees without unmounting them, enabling features like tab switching and background rendering. --- ## Overview: Boundary and Visibility Mechanisms React provides three core primitives that interrupt or modify the normal rendering flow of a component tree: | Mechanism | Purpose | Primary Data Structure | Key Logic File | |-----------|---------|------------------------|----------------| | **Suspense Boundary** | Handles thrown promises/async data | `SuspenseState` | `ReactFiberSuspenseComponent.js` | | **Error Boundary** | Handles JS errors in class components | `UpdateQueue` (Capture) | `ReactFiberClassComponent.js` | | **Offscreen / Activity** | Manages visibility and prerendering | `OffscreenState` | `ReactFiberOffscreenComponent.js` | Sources: [packages/react-reconciler/src/ReactWorkTags.js:143-148](), [packages/react-reconciler/src/ReactFiberBeginWork.js:71-85]() --- ## Suspense Boundaries ### Mechanics of Suspending When a component needs data that isn't ready, it "suspends" by throwing a `Wakeable` (typically a Promise). The reconciler catches this in the work loop and initiates the "unwind" phase to find the nearest boundary. Title: Suspense Throw and Catch Data Flow ```mermaid graph TD "Component[Fiber]" -- "throws Wakeable" --> "handleThrow()[ReactFiberWorkLoop.js]" "handleThrow()" --> "throwException()[ReactFiberThrow.js]" "throwException()" -- "finds" --> "SuspenseHandler[SuspenseContext.js]" "SuspenseHandler" -- "marks" --> "ShouldCapture[ReactFiberFlags.js]" "WorkLoop" -- "calls" --> "unwindWork()[ReactFiberUnwindWork.js]" "unwindWork()" -- "transforms" --> "DidCapture[ReactFiberFlags.js]" "Re-render" -- "renders" --> "fallback[SuspenseProps]" ``` Sources: [packages/react-reconciler/src/ReactFiberWorkLoop.js:1800-1900](), [packages/react-reconciler/src/ReactFiberThrow.js:238-242](), [packages/react-reconciler/src/ReactFiberUnwindWork.js:156-183]() ### Suspense State and Retries A `SuspenseComponent` fiber maintains a `memoizedState` of type `SuspenseState`. This tracks whether the boundary is currently showing a fallback or dehydrated content. - **`SuspenseState`**: Contains `dehydrated` (for SSR), `retryLane` (priority for re-render), and `treeContext`. [packages/react-reconciler/src/ReactFiberSuspenseComponent.js:24-36]() - **Retry Mechanism**: When a thrown promise resolves, `pingSuspendedRoot` is called, which uses `markRootPinged` to notify the scheduler that the suspended lane is ready to be retried. [packages/react-reconciler/src/ReactFiberWorkLoop.js:203-204](), [packages/react-reconciler/src/ReactFiberThrow.js:400-500]() --- ## Error Boundaries Error boundaries are implemented via Class Components using `getDerivedStateFromError` (for state updates) and `componentDidCatch` (for side effects). ### Error Capture Implementation When an error is thrown, `throwException` identifies the nearest Class Component with error lifecycles. It creates a `CaptureUpdate` and enqueues it into the fiber's `updateQueue`. Title: Error Boundary Entity Mapping ```mermaid graph LR subgraph "Logic Space" "throwException()" -- "creates" --> "CaptureUpdate" "unwindWork()" -- "identifies" --> "ClassComponent" end subgraph "Code Entities" "CaptureUpdate" -- "is" --> "Update[ReactFiberClassUpdateQueue.js]" "ClassComponent" -- "uses" --> "getDerivedStateFromError[ReactFiberClassComponent.js]" "CommitPhase" -- "calls" --> "componentDidCatch[ReactFiberCommitWork.js]" end ``` Sources: [packages/react-reconciler/src/ReactFiberThrow.js:239-242](), [packages/react-reconciler/src/ReactFiberClassUpdateQueue.js:150-152](), [packages/react-reconciler/src/ReactFiberCommitWork.js:227-232]() --- ## Offscreen and Activity Components The `Offscreen` component (exposed internally as `Activity`) allows React to render a tree but keep it "hidden" from the host environment (e.g., the DOM). ### Offscreen States and Visibility Offscreen fibers use `OffscreenState` to track visibility. When a tree is hidden, React does not unmount it; instead, it toggles the `Visibility` flag. - **`OffscreenVisible`**: The tree is active and visible. - **`OffscreenDetached`**: The tree is hidden. Its passive effects (like `useEffect`) are unmounted, but its state is preserved. Sources: [packages/react-reconciler/src/ReactFiberOffscreenComponent.js:68-80](), [packages/react-reconciler/src/ReactFiberBeginWork.js:79-85]() ### Prerendering and Low Priority Offscreen allows for "prerendering" at a lower priority (`OffscreenLane`). If a tree is hidden, React can still perform work on it using background cycles without blocking the main UI thread. - **`pushOffscreenSuspenseHandler`**: Offscreen acts as a special kind of suspense handler that can defer rendering if children suspend. [packages/react-reconciler/src/ReactFiberSuspenseContext.js:203-204]() - **`Visibility` Flag**: During `completeWork`, if an Offscreen component's visibility changes, React marks it with the `Visibility` flag, which the commit phase uses to hide/show host instances. [packages/react-reconciler/src/ReactFiberCompleteWork.js:88-100](), [packages/react-reconciler/src/ReactFiberCommitWork.js:245-247]() --- ## Hydration and Dehydrated Boundaries Suspense boundaries play a critical role in streaming SSR. A "dehydrated" boundary represents server-rendered HTML that hasn't been made interactive yet. - **`reenterHydrationStateFromDehydratedSuspenseInstance`**: Used when the reconciler starts hydrating a Suspense boundary. [packages/react-reconciler/src/ReactFiberHydrationContext.js:243-244]() - **`claimNextHydratableSuspenseInstance`**: The reconciler attempts to match a Suspense fiber to a `SuspenseInstance` (marker) in the DOM. [packages/react-reconciler/src/ReactFiberHydrationContext.js:249-250]() ### Hydration Data Flow 1. **BeginWork**: Enters hydration state for the boundary. [packages/react-reconciler/src/ReactFiberBeginWork.js:241-243]() 2. **CompleteWork**: Validates if the hydrated content matches the fiber. If a mismatch occurs, it triggers `ForceClientRender`. [packages/react-reconciler/src/ReactFiberCompleteWork.js:156-165]() 3. **CommitWork**: If successful, the boundary is marked as hydrated. [packages/react-reconciler/src/ReactFiberCommitWork.js:241-242]() Sources: [packages/react-reconciler/src/ReactFiberHydrationContext.js:160-165](), [packages/react-reconciler/src/ReactFiberCompleteWork.js:156-165]() --- ## Key Functions Summary | Function | Role | Location | |----------|------|----------| | `throwException` | Main entry point for handling thrown values (errors/promises). | `ReactFiberThrow.js` | | `unwindWork` | Pops context stack and finds boundaries during an exception. | `ReactFiberUnwindWork.js` | | `handleThrow` | Work loop integration for catching and restarting after a throw. | `ReactFiberWorkLoop.js` | | `pushPrimaryTreeSuspenseHandler` | Manages the stack of active suspense boundaries. | `ReactFiberSuspenseContext.js` | | `commitShowHideSuspenseBoundary` | Mutates the host UI to show/hide fallback. | `ReactFiberCommitWork.js` | Sources: [packages/react-reconciler/src/ReactFiberThrow.js:238-242](), [packages/react-reconciler/src/ReactFiberUnwindWork.js:66-75](), [packages/react-reconciler/src/ReactFiberWorkLoop.js:1800-1900](), [packages/react-reconciler/src/ReactFiberCommitWork.js:245-247]() --- # Page: Profiling and Performance Tracking # Profiling and Performance Tracking

[packages/react/src/__tests__/ReactProfiler-test.internal.js](packages/react/src/__tests__/ReactProfiler-test.internal.js) - [packages/shared/ReactPerformanceTrackProperties.js](packages/shared/ReactPerformanceTrackProperties.js)
This document describes React's profiling and performance tracking infrastructure, which measures render performance and integrates with developer tools. The system tracks timing metrics during rendering and committing, exposes a user-facing `` component, and logs performance data to Chrome DevTools Performance Panel and React DevTools. For information about the general rendering process, see [Work Loop and Rendering Phases](4.2). For DevTools backend architecture, see [DevTools Backend Architecture](7.1). --- ## Purpose and Scope The profiling system serves multiple purposes: 1. **User-facing profiling** via the `` component that measures render times. 2. **Internal performance tracking** that records timing metrics on Fiber nodes. 3. **Chrome Performance Panel integration** via `performance.measure()` and `console.timeStamp()` APIs. 4. **React DevTools Profiler** data collection for visualizing component performance. 5. **Performance issue detection** for detecting long renders, cascading updates, and deep equality issues. The system is controlled by feature flags `enableProfilerTimer`, `enableProfilerCommitHooks`, `enableComponentPerformanceTrack`, and `enableSchedulingProfiler` [packages/shared/ReactFeatureFlags.js:45-55](). --- ## System Architecture ### Profiling Flow Through Render and Commit ```mermaid graph TD subgraph "Application Code" UserComponent["User Component"] ProfilerComponent[""] end subgraph "Render Phase" BeginWork["beginWork()
ReactFiberBeginWork.js"] StartTimer["startProfilerTimer()
ReactProfilerTimer.js"] RenderHooks["renderWithHooks()
ReactFiberHooks.js"] StopTimer["stopProfilerTimerIfRunning()
ReactProfilerTimer.js"] MarkRender["markComponentRenderStarted/Stopped()
ReactFiberDevToolsHook.js"] end subgraph "Complete Phase" CompleteWork["completeWork()
ReactFiberCompleteWork.js"] TransferDuration["transferActualDuration()
ReactProfilerTimer.js"] end subgraph "Commit Phase" CommitLayout["commitLayoutEffects()
ReactFiberCommitWork.js"] ProfilerUpdate["commitProfilerUpdate()
ReactFiberCommitEffects.js"] ProfilerPostCommit["commitProfilerPostCommit()
ReactFiberCommitEffects.js"] EffectTracking["pushNestedEffectDurations()
ReactProfilerTimer.js"] end subgraph "Output" OnRenderCallback["onRender callback"] DevTools["React DevTools"] PerfPanel["Chrome Performance Panel"] end UserComponent --> ProfilerComponent ProfilerComponent --> BeginWork BeginWork --> StartTimer BeginWork --> MarkRender BeginWork --> RenderHooks RenderHooks --> StopTimer BeginWork --> CompleteWork CompleteWork --> TransferDuration TransferDuration --> CommitLayout CommitLayout --> EffectTracking CommitLayout --> ProfilerUpdate ProfilerUpdate --> ProfilerPostCommit ProfilerPostCommit --> OnRenderCallback ProfilerPostCommit --> DevTools MarkRender --> PerfPanel ``` **Diagram: End-to-end profiling flow from user code through render and commit phases** Sources: [packages/react-reconciler/src/ReactFiberBeginWork.js:70](), [packages/react-reconciler/src/ReactFiberCompleteWork.js:184](), [packages/react-reconciler/src/ReactFiberCommitWork.js:235-236](), [packages/react-reconciler/src/ReactProfilerTimer.js:1-100]() --- ## The Profiler Component ### User-Facing API The `` component measures rendering performance of its descendants [packages/react-reconciler/src/ReactWorkTags.js:70](). The `onRender` callback receives timing information [packages/react/src/__tests__/ReactProfiler-test.internal.js:68-102](): ```javascript function onRender( id, // string: Profiler id prop phase, // "mount" | "update" | "nested-update" actualDuration, // number: Time spent rendering the update baseDuration, // number: Estimated time to render the entire subtree startTime, // number: When React began rendering this update commitTime, // number: When React committed this update ) { // Log or aggregate performance data } ``` ### Profiler Fiber Implementation When the reconciler encounters a `Profiler` tag in `beginWork`, it handles the component by pushing timing context [packages/react-reconciler/src/ReactFiberBeginWork.js:1008-1033](). | Fiber Property | Description | | :--- | :--- | | `actualDuration` | Time spent rendering the fiber and its descendants in the current update [packages/react-reconciler/src/ReactFiber.js:193](). | | `actualStartTime` | Timestamp when the fiber started rendering [packages/react-reconciler/src/ReactFiber.js:194](). | | `selfBaseDuration` | Duration to render the fiber itself [packages/react-reconciler/src/ReactFiber.js:195](). | | `treeBaseDuration` | Duration to render the fiber and all its descendants [packages/react-reconciler/src/ReactFiber.js:196](). | Sources: [packages/react-reconciler/src/ReactFiber.js:179-197](), [packages/react-reconciler/src/ReactFiberBeginWork.js:1008-1033]() --- ## Internal Timing Infrastructure ### ReactProfilerTimer Implementation The `ReactProfilerTimer` module manages the state of the profiling clock. It uses `Scheduler.now()` to get high-resolution timestamps [packages/react-reconciler/src/ReactProfilerTimer.js:46](). Key functions include: * `startProfilerTimer(fiber)`: Sets `actualStartTime` on the fiber [packages/react-reconciler/src/ReactProfilerTimer.js:413-424](). * `stopProfilerTimerIfRunningAndRecordDuration(fiber)`: Calculates elapsed time and adds it to `actualDuration` [packages/react-reconciler/src/ReactProfilerTimer.js:520-551](). * `transferActualDuration(fiber)`: Bubbles up durations from children to parents during the complete phase [packages/react-reconciler/src/ReactProfilerTimer.js:553-574](). ### Duration Calculation #### actualDuration vs. baseDuration 1. **actualDuration**: The sum of time spent in the `beginWork` and `completeWork` phases for a fiber and its sub-tree during a specific render [packages/react-reconciler/src/ReactProfilerTimer.js:520-551](). 2. **baseDuration**: An estimate of the time it would take to render the sub-tree without any memoization. It is calculated by summing the `selfBaseDuration` of all descendants [packages/react-reconciler/src/ReactProfilerTimer.js:576-609](). Sources: [packages/react-reconciler/src/ReactProfilerTimer.js:520-609]() --- ## Performance Track Logging System React includes a "Performance Track" system that emits events to the browser's performance timeline (visible in Chrome DevTools Performance Panel) [packages/react-reconciler/src/ReactFiberPerformanceTrack.js:42-50](). ### Track Architecture The system organizes logs into two main tracks: 1. **Components ⚛**: Individual component lifecycles (Mount, Render, Effect) [packages/react-reconciler/src/ReactFiberPerformanceTrack.js:52](). 2. **Scheduler ⚛**: Lane-based tracks (Blocking, Gesture, Transition, Suspense, Idle) [packages/react-reconciler/src/ReactFiberPerformanceTrack.js:53](). ```mermaid graph LR subgraph "React Performance Track" direction TB LanesTrack["Scheduler ⚛ Track Group"] CompTrack["Components ⚛ Track"] end subgraph "Code Entities" WorkLoop["ReactFiberWorkLoop.js"] PerfTrack["ReactFiberPerformanceTrack.js"] Lanes["ReactFiberLane.js"] end WorkLoop -- "setCurrentTrackFromLanes()" --> PerfTrack PerfTrack -- "getGroupNameOfHighestPriorityLane()" --> Lanes PerfTrack -- "console.timeStamp()" --> LanesTrack PerfTrack -- "performance.measure()" --> CompTrack ``` **Diagram: Mapping of Performance Track logic to Code Entities** Sources: [packages/react-reconciler/src/ReactFiberPerformanceTrack.js:52-110](), [packages/react-reconciler/src/ReactFiberWorkLoop.js:99-102]() ### Logged Operations | Function | Code Entity | Description | | :--- | :--- | :--- | | `logComponentRender` | `ReactFiberPerformanceTrack.js` | Logs a component render event with duration and changed props [packages/react-reconciler/src/ReactFiberPerformanceTrack.js:221-350](). | | `logComponentEffect` | `ReactFiberPerformanceTrack.js` | Logs time spent in `useEffect` or `useLayoutEffect` [packages/react-reconciler/src/ReactFiberPerformanceTrack.js:462-520](). | | `logTransitionStart` | `ReactFiberPerformanceTrack.js` | Marks the start of a Transition lane update [packages/react-reconciler/src/ReactFiberPerformanceTrack.js:61-110](). | Sources: [packages/react-reconciler/src/ReactFiberPerformanceTrack.js:221-520]() --- ## DevTools Profiling Hooks React exposes internal hooks for the DevTools extension to instrument the reconciler. These are injected via `injectProfilingHooks` [packages/react-reconciler/src/ReactFiberDevToolsHook.js:66](). ### Instrumentation Points ```mermaid sequenceDiagram participant Reconciler as ReactFiberWorkLoop.js participant Hooks as ReactFiberDevToolsHook.js participant DevTools as DevTools Backend Reconciler->>Hooks: markRenderStarted(lanes) Hooks->>DevTools: emit('render-started') Reconciler->>Hooks: markComponentRenderStarted(fiber) Hooks->>DevTools: emit('component-render-started') Reconciler->>Hooks: onCommitRoot(root) Hooks->>DevTools: emit('commit-root') ``` **Diagram: Reconciler instrumentation via DevTools hooks** The hooks track: * **Scheduling**: When a render is scheduled [packages/react-reconciler/src/ReactFiberDevToolsHook.js:64](). * **Render Phase**: Component-level start/stop times [packages/react-reconciler/src/ReactFiberBeginWork.js:52-53](). * **Commit Phase**: Time spent in layout and passive effects [packages/react-reconciler/src/ReactFiberCommitWork.js:208](). Sources: [packages/react-reconciler/src/ReactFiberDevToolsHook.js:52-67](), [packages/react-reconciler/src/ReactFiberBeginWork.js:52-55]() --- ## Implementation Details: Effect Timing The system tracks effect durations separately from render durations to provide a complete picture of component overhead. 1. **Start**: `pushComponentEffectStart()` is called before executing an effect [packages/react-reconciler/src/ReactProfilerTimer.js:651-665](). 2. **End**: `popComponentEffectStart()` records the timestamp after execution [packages/react-reconciler/src/ReactProfilerTimer.js:667-681](). 3. **Aggregation**: Durations are bubbled up to the nearest `Profiler` boundary using `pushNestedEffectDurations` [packages/react-reconciler/src/ReactProfilerTimer.js:230-244](). Sources: [packages/react-reconciler/src/ReactProfilerTimer.js:651-681](), [packages/react-reconciler/src/ReactFiberCommitWork.js:124-141]() --- # Page: Server-Side Rendering # Server-Side Rendering

This page introduces the two modern server rendering systems in the React codebase—**Fizz** and **Flight**—and explains their purposes, entry points, and how they interact. It is a navigation starting point; detailed internals are in the child pages: - [React Fizz (Streaming SSR)](#5.1) — Streaming HTML engine. - [React Server Components (Flight)](#5.2) — Server-to-client serialization protocol. - [Server Actions and Form Handling](#5.3) — Bidirectional communication and form status hooks. - [Hydration System](#5.4) — Client-side reattachment of event handlers and state. --- ## Two Distinct Systems React ships two independent server rendering systems, both housed inside the `react-server` package but with entirely different output formats and architectures. | Aspect | **Fizz** | **Flight** | |---|---|---| | **Purpose** | Render a React tree to HTML for initial page load | Serialize a server component tree for consumption by clients | | **Output** | HTML text stream | Custom wire protocol (newline-delimited rows) | | **Suspense** | Writes a placeholder; later streams inline `

This document introduces the `facebook/react` repository, explaining its purpose as a JavaScript library for building user interfaces and providing a high-level overview of its architecture. The repository contains the React reconciler, multiple platform renderers, server-side rendering systems, and a sophisticated build pipeline. --- ## What is React? React (current version **19.3.0**, [packages/shared/ReactVersion.js:15]()) is a JavaScript library for building user interfaces through a declarative component model. The repository is a monorepo managed with Yarn workspaces [package.json:2-5]() that ships: - **Core library** (`react`): Component API, hooks (`useState`, `useEffect`, etc.), and the JSX runtime [packages/react/package.json:1-52](). - **Platform renderers**: `react-dom` for the web [packages/react-dom/package.json:1-127](), `react-native-renderer` for mobile, and `react-art` for vector graphics [packages/react-art/package.json:1-42](). - **Reconciler** (`react-reconciler`): The Fiber architecture engine that manages the component tree and updates [packages/react-reconciler/package.json:1-30](). - **Scheduler** (`scheduler`): A cooperative task scheduler for prioritizing work [packages/scheduler/package.json:1-25](). - **Server rendering**: Systems for streaming HTML (Fizz) and React Server Components (Flight). - **React Compiler**: An experimental build-time tool for automatic memoization [package.json:125](). Sources: [packages/shared/ReactVersion.js:15](), [package.json:2-5](), [packages/react/package.json:1-52](), [packages/react-dom/package.json:1-127](), [packages/react-art/package.json:1-42]() --- ## High-Level Architecture **Diagram: Overall System Architecture** ```mermaid graph TB subgraph BuildSystem["Build System (scripts/rollup/)"] Bundles["bundles.js
