useState, useEffect, etc."] end subgraph Renderers["Platform Renderers"] ReactDOM["react-dom
Web/DOM"] ReactNative["react-native-renderer
Fabric & Paper"] ReactART["react-art"] TestRenderer["react-test-renderer"] end subgraph ServerSide["Server Rendering"] Fizz["Fizz
Streaming HTML"] Flight["Flight
Server Components"] end subgraph DevTools["Developer Tools"] DevToolsShared["react-devtools-shared"] ESLintPlugin["eslint-plugin-react-hooks"] Compiler["babel-plugin-react-compiler"] end Build --> Bundles Bundles --> Forks Forks --> BaseFlags BaseFlags --> WWWFlags BaseFlags --> NativeFBFlags BaseFlags --> NativeOSSFlags React --> Reconciler Reconciler --> Scheduler Reconciler --> Hooks Reconciler --> ReactDOM Reconciler --> ReactNative Reconciler --> ReactART Reconciler --> TestRenderer Fizz --> ReactDOM Flight --> Fizz DevToolsShared -.->|"inspects"| Reconciler ESLintPlugin -.->|"lints"| React Compiler -.->|"transforms"| React ``` Sources: [scripts/rollup/build.js:1-50](), [scripts/rollup/bundles.js:1-152](), [scripts/rollup/forks.js:1-61](), [packages/shared/ReactVersion.js:15]() --- ## Repository Layout The root is a Yarn workspace monorepo [package.json:2-5](). Most logic resides in `packages/`, while the build and test infrastructure is located in `scripts/`. ``` / ├── packages/ # All npm packages (react, react-dom, scheduler, etc.) ├── compiler/ # React Compiler (separate workspace) ├── scripts/ │ ├── rollup/ # Build pipeline (build.js, bundles.js, forks.js) │ ├── jest/ # Test runner and configuration │ ├── flow/ # Flow type configuration [scripts/flow/config/flowconfig:1-44] │ └── shared/ # Shared build helpers ├── fixtures/ # Integration test fixtures └── package.json # Root workspace configuration ``` For details on how these packages relate, see [Repository Structure and Packages](#1.1). Sources: [package.json:1-150](), [scripts/rollup/bundles.js:1-100](), [scripts/flow/config/flowconfig:1-44]() --- ## Key Subsystems ### Reconciler and Renderers The **Reconciler** (`react-reconciler`) is the platform-agnostic core that implements the Fiber architecture. It communicates with **Renderers** (like `react-dom` or `react-native-renderer`) via a `HostConfig` [scripts/rollup/forks.js:242-274](). This allows React to target different environments while sharing the same diffing and scheduling logic. ### Server-Side Rendering (SSR) React provides two modern SSR systems: - **Fizz**: A streaming HTML renderer used by `react-dom/server` [packages/react-dom/package.json:51-70](). - **Flight**: The protocol and implementation for **React Server Components (RSC)**, allowing components to fetch data and run exclusively on the server. ### Build System and Forking React uses a sophisticated build pipeline based on Rollup [scripts/rollup/build.js:1-30](). A key feature is **Module Forking**, defined in `forks.js` [scripts/rollup/forks.js:52-191](). This system allows the build to swap out implementation files (like `ReactFeatureFlags.js`) based on the target platform (e.g., `www` vs `native`). **Diagram: Build Pipeline and Module Forking** ```mermaid graph TB subgraph Source["Source Code"] Shared["packages/shared/ReactFeatureFlags.js"] end subgraph Config["Build Config"] Bundles["bundles.js
Define targets: FB_WWW, RN_OSS"] Forks["forks.js
findNearestExistingForkFile"] end subgraph Pipeline["Rollup Pipeline"] UseForks["use-forks-plugin.js"] Babel["@rollup/plugin-babel"] Closure["google-closure-compiler"] end subgraph Output["Output Bundles"] WWW["build/facebook-www"] OSS["build/node_modules"] end Shared --> UseForks Bundles --> UseForks Forks --> UseForks UseForks --> Babel Babel --> Closure Closure --> WWW Closure --> OSS ``` Sources: [scripts/rollup/build.js:1-30](), [scripts/rollup/bundles.js:1-152](), [scripts/rollup/forks.js:1-61](), [scripts/rollup/forks.js:29-43]() --- ## Child Pages - [Repository Structure and Packages](#1.1) — Detailed breakdown of the monorepo packages and their dependencies. Sources: [package.json:1-5](), [scripts/rollup/bundles.js:56-67]() --- # Page: Repository Structure and Packages # Repository Structure and Packages

## Purpose and Scope This document describes the monorepo structure of the React repository, cataloging all packages, their purposes, dependencies, and how they are organized for building and distribution. It covers the physical directory layout, `package.json` configurations, and the relationships between packages. The React repository is managed as a monorepo using Yarn Workspaces, allowing multiple packages to reside in a single repository while sharing dependencies and build infrastructure. --- ## Monorepo Organization The React repository is structured as a Yarn workspace monorepo. The root [package.json:3-5]() declares the workspace configuration: ```json "workspaces": [ "packages/*" ] ``` All source code for publishable packages resides in the `packages/` directory. The build system outputs artifacts to `build/node_modules/` for npm distribution, or specific subdirectories like `build/facebook-www/` and `build/oss-experimental/` for internal or canary targets [package.json:130-131](). **Sources:** [package.json:1-150]() --- ## Package Categories and Module Types The build system classifies packages into specific module types to determine how they are bundled and which dependencies are inlined. These types are defined in [scripts/rollup/bundles.js:56-65](): | Module Type | Description | Example | |:---|:---|:---| | `ISOMORPHIC` | Core React logic that is platform-agnostic. | `react` | | `RENDERER` | Platform-specific implementations that bundle the reconciler. | `react-dom`, `react-native-renderer` | | `RENDERER_UTILS` | Helper packages that access a renderer's internals for testing. | `react-dom/test-utils` | | `RECONCILER` | Standalone reconciler for third-party renderers. | `react-reconciler` | ### Package Category Diagram This diagram associates high-level package roles with their corresponding directory structures and entry points as defined in the build configuration [scripts/rollup/bundles.js:69-1103](). ```mermaid graph TB subgraph "ISOMORPHIC (Core Logic)" react["react
(packages/react)"] reactServer["react.react-server
(react/src/ReactServer.js)"] jsxRuntime["jsx-runtime
(react/jsx-runtime)"] compilerRuntime["compiler-runtime
(react/compiler-runtime)"] end subgraph "RENDERER (Platform Specific)" reactDOM["react-dom
(packages/react-dom)"] reactNative["react-native-renderer
(packages/react-native-renderer)"] reactArt["react-art
(packages/react-art)"] reactTestRenderer["react-test-renderer
(packages/react-test-renderer)"] end subgraph "RECONCILER (Engine)" reconciler["react-reconciler
(packages/react-reconciler)"] end subgraph "RENDERER_UTILS & Support" testUtils["react-dom/test-utils"] scheduler["scheduler
(packages/scheduler)"] reactIs["react-is
(packages/react-is)"] compiler["babel-plugin-react-compiler
(packages/babel-plugin-react-compiler)"] end reactDOM -- "Inlines" --> reconciler reactNative -- "Inlines" --> reconciler reconciler -- "Uses" --> scheduler ``` **Sources:** [scripts/rollup/bundles.js:56-67](), [scripts/rollup/bundles.js:69-1103]() --- ## Core Packages ### react **Location:** `packages/react/` **Module Type:** `ISOMORPHIC` **Version:** 19.3.0 The core React package exports the public API (e.g., `useState`, `useEffect`, `createElement`) used by developers. It contains no platform-specific logic (no DOM or Native references). **Key Exports** ([packages/react/package.json:24-42]()): - `.` → Main entry point (`index.js`). - `./jsx-runtime` → Production JSX transformation runtime. - `./jsx-dev-runtime` → Development JSX transformation runtime. - `./compiler-runtime` → Specialized runtime for code generated by the React Compiler. - `react-server` condition → A subset of React APIs safe for use in React Server Components (`react.react-server.js`). **Sources:** [packages/react/package.json:1-52](), [scripts/rollup/bundles.js:71-88]() ### react-dom **Location:** `packages/react-dom/` **Module Type:** `RENDERER` **Version:** 19.3.0 The DOM renderer for React. It implements the `HostConfig` for the web platform and manages the reconciliation of React components into DOM elements. **Key Exports** ([packages/react-dom/package.json:51-125]()): - `./client` → Modern client APIs like `createRoot` and `hydrateRoot`. - `./server` → Streaming SSR APIs (Fizz). It provides different entry points for various runtimes: `server.node.js`, `server.browser.js`, `server.edge.js`, and `server.bun.js`. - `./static` → Static site generation (SSG) APIs. - `./test-utils` → Utilities for testing DOM components. **Dependencies:** - `scheduler` (direct) [packages/react-dom/package.json:19-21](). - `react` (peer) [packages/react-dom/package.json:22-24](). **Sources:** [packages/react-dom/package.json:1-126](), [scripts/rollup/bundles.js:180-423]() ### react-reconciler **Location:** `packages/react-reconciler/` **Module Type:** `RECONCILER` **Version:** 0.34.0 The core "engine" of React. It manages the Fiber tree, diffing, and scheduling. It is platform-agnostic and requires a `HostConfig` to be injected to perform actual updates. The reconciler is typically consumed by renderers. In production builds, it is wrapped in a factory function [scripts/rollup/wrappers.js:189-198](): ```javascript module.exports = function $$$reconciler($$$config) { var exports = {}; // Reconciler implementation using $$$config return exports; }; ``` **Sources:** [packages/react-reconciler/package.json:1-34](), [scripts/rollup/wrappers.js:171-263]() --- ## Supporting and Utility Packages ### scheduler **Location:** `packages/scheduler/` **Version:** 0.28.0 A cooperative task scheduler used by React for time-slicing and prioritization. It handles the scheduling of work based on different priority levels (e.g., `ImmediatePriority`, `UserBlockingPriority`). **Sources:** [packages/scheduler/package.json:1-33](), [scripts/rollup/bundles.js:1008-1096]() ### react-is **Location:** `packages/react-is/` **Version:** 19.3.0 A utility package for identifying React element types (e.g., checking if a value is a `Fragment`, `Portal`, or `Memo` component). **Sources:** [packages/react-is/package.json:1-26]() ### react-art **Location:** `packages/react-art/` **Version:** 19.3.0 A renderer for drawing vector graphics using React, supporting Canvas, SVG, or VML [packages/react-art/package.json:2-3](). **Sources:** [packages/react-art/package.json:1-41]() --- ## Testing Infrastructure Packages ### react-test-renderer **Location:** `packages/react-test-renderer/` **Version:** 19.3.0 Provides a renderer that can be used to render React components to pure JavaScript objects, without depending on the DOM or a native mobile environment. This is primarily used for snapshot testing [packages/react-test-renderer/package.json:4](). **Sources:** [packages/react-test-renderer/package.json:1-35]() ### react-noop-renderer **Location:** `packages/react-noop-renderer/` An internal-only renderer used for testing the reconciler and core React logic. It "renders" to an in-memory data structure, allowing tests to assert on the state of the tree without any platform overhead. **Sources:** [packages/react-noop-renderer/package.json:1-32]() --- ## Module Forking and Environment Resolution React uses a "forking" system during the build process to swap out implementation files based on the target environment (e.g., `www` for Facebook's internal web stack, `native` for React Native). ### Forking Resolution Diagram This diagram shows how the build script `build.js` uses `forks.js` to map generic source paths to environment-specific files during the Rollup bundling process. ```mermaid graph LR subgraph "Source Code Space" featureFlags["shared/ReactFeatureFlags.js"] sharedInternals["shared/ReactSharedInternals.js"] end subgraph "Forking Logic (scripts/rollup/forks.js)" resolver["forks.js resolver"] end subgraph "Code Entity Space (Forks)" wwwFlags["ReactFeatureFlags.www.js"] nativeFlags["ReactFeatureFlags.native-fb.js"] serverInternals["ReactSharedInternalsServer.js"] end featureFlags -- "bundleType: FB_WWW" --> resolver resolver -- "Maps to" --> wwwFlags featureFlags -- "bundleType: RN_FB" --> resolver resolver -- "Maps to" --> nativeFlags sharedInternals -- "entry: ReactServer.js" --> resolver resolver -- "Maps to" --> serverInternals ``` **Key Forking Rules** ([scripts/rollup/forks.js:52-191]()): - **Feature Flags:** `ReactFeatureFlags.js` is forked into `.www.js`, `.native-fb.js`, or `.native-oss.js` depending on the `bundleType` [scripts/rollup/forks.js:134-191](). - **Shared Internals:** `ReactSharedInternals.js` is forked to prevent circular dependencies. If the entry point is `react`, it uses `ReactSharedInternalsClient.js` [scripts/rollup/forks.js:62-63](). **Sources:** [scripts/rollup/forks.js:1-191](), [scripts/rollup/build.js:22-25]() --- ## Build System Integration The repository uses Rollup for bundling. The build process is driven by `scripts/rollup/build.js`, which consumes bundle definitions from `scripts/rollup/bundles.js`. ### Bundle Configuration Table Examples of how different packages are mapped to bundle types and entry points [scripts/rollup/bundles.js:69-1103](): | Package | Entry Point | Module Type | Bundle Types | |:---|:---|:---|:---| | `react` | `react` | `ISOMORPHIC` | `NODE_DEV`, `NODE_PROD`, `FB_WWW_DEV`, `RN_FB_DEV`, etc. | | `react-dom` | `react-dom` | `RENDERER` | `NODE_DEV`, `NODE_PROD` | | `react-dom (www)`| `react-dom/src/ReactDOMFB.js`| `RENDERER` | `FB_WWW_DEV`, `FB_WWW_PROD` | | `scheduler` | `scheduler` | `ISOMORPHIC` | `NODE_DEV`, `NODE_PROD`, `FB_WWW_DEV` | **Sources:** [scripts/rollup/bundles.js:69-1103](), [scripts/rollup/build.js:1-100]() --- # Page: Feature Flags System # Feature Flags System
Relevant source files The following files were used as context for generating this wiki page: - [packages/shared/ReactFeatureFlags.js](packages/shared/ReactFeatureFlags.js) - [packages/shared/forks/ReactFeatureFlags.native-fb-dynamic.js](packages/shared/forks/ReactFeatureFlags.native-fb-dynamic.js) - [packages/shared/forks/ReactFeatureFlags.native-fb.js](packages/shared/forks/ReactFeatureFlags.native-fb.js) - [packages/shared/forks/ReactFeatureFlags.native-oss.js](packages/shared/forks/ReactFeatureFlags.native-oss.js) - [packages/shared/forks/ReactFeatureFlags.test-renderer.js](packages/shared/forks/ReactFeatureFlags.test-renderer.js) - [packages/shared/forks/ReactFeatureFlags.test-renderer.native-fb.js](packages/shared/forks/ReactFeatureFlags.test-renderer.native-fb.js) - [packages/shared/forks/ReactFeatureFlags.test-renderer.www.js](packages/shared/forks/ReactFeatureFlags.test-renderer.www.js) - [packages/shared/forks/ReactFeatureFlags.www-dynamic.js](packages/shared/forks/ReactFeatureFlags.www-dynamic.js) - [packages/shared/forks/ReactFeatureFlags.www.js](packages/shared/forks/ReactFeatureFlags.www.js) - [scripts/flow/xplat.js](scripts/flow/xplat.js)
## Purpose and Scope The Feature Flags System is React's comprehensive mechanism for controlling experimental features, platform-specific behavior, and gradual feature rollouts across different environments and release channels. It enables the same codebase to be compiled with different behaviors for Facebook's internal infrastructure (www), React Native Facebook internal (native-fb), React Native open source (native-oss), and test renderers, while also supporting A/B testing and runtime-controllable flags in production environments. This document covers the flag definition structure, forking mechanism, dynamic flag integration, build-time macros, and flag lifecycle management. ## System Architecture The feature flag system is structured as a base definition file with environment-specific forks that override default values. The system supports both static flags (determined at build time) and dynamic flags (controllable at runtime via gatekeepers or feature management systems). ### Architectural Overview ```mermaid graph TB subgraph "Base Definitions" BaseFlags["ReactFeatureFlags.js
Canonical flag definitions"] end subgraph "Static Forks" WWWFork["ReactFeatureFlags.www.js
Facebook internal"] NativeFBFork["ReactFeatureFlags.native-fb.js
React Native FB"] NativeOSSFork["ReactFeatureFlags.native-oss.js
React Native OSS"] TestRendererFork["ReactFeatureFlags.test-renderer.js
Test renderer"] TestRendererWWWFork["ReactFeatureFlags.test-renderer.www.js
Test renderer www"] TestRendererNativeFBFork["ReactFeatureFlags.test-renderer.native-fb.js
Test renderer native-fb"] end subgraph "Dynamic Forks" WWWDynamic["ReactFeatureFlags.www-dynamic.js
Gatekeeper integration"] NativeFBDynamic["ReactFeatureFlags.native-fb-dynamic.js
Native feature flags"] end subgraph "Build-Time Macros" ExperimentalMacro["__EXPERIMENTAL__
Experimental channel"] ProfileMacro["__PROFILE__
Profiling builds"] VariantMacro["__VARIANT__
A/B testing"] end subgraph "Runtime Integration" Gatekeeper["Gatekeeper System
Facebook infrastructure"] ReactNativeFlags["ReactNativeInternalFeatureFlags
Native module"] end BaseFlags --> WWWFork BaseFlags --> NativeFBFork BaseFlags --> NativeOSSFork BaseFlags --> TestRendererFork BaseFlags --> TestRendererWWWFork BaseFlags --> TestRendererNativeFBFork WWWFork --> WWWDynamic NativeFBFork --> NativeFBDynamic WWWDynamic --> Gatekeeper NativeFBDynamic --> ReactNativeFlags ExperimentalMacro --> WWWFork ExperimentalMacro --> NativeFBFork ExperimentalMacro --> BaseFlags ProfileMacro --> WWWFork ProfileMacro --> NativeFBFork ProfileMacro --> BaseFlags VariantMacro --> WWWDynamic VariantMacro --> NativeFBDynamic ``` **Sources:** [packages/shared/ReactFeatureFlags.js:1-259](), [packages/shared/forks/ReactFeatureFlags.www.js:1-127](), [packages/shared/forks/ReactFeatureFlags.native-fb.js:1-98](), [packages/shared/forks/ReactFeatureFlags.native-oss.js:1-98]() ## Base Feature Flag Definitions The base feature flag definitions reside in `ReactFeatureFlags.js` and serve as the canonical source of all flags with their default values for open source builds. This file organizes flags into explicit lifecycle categories. ### Flag Organization Structure ```mermaid graph LR BaseFlags["ReactFeatureFlags.js"] BaseFlags --> LandOrRemoveZero["Land or remove (zero effort)
Lines 10-17"] BaseFlags --> Killswitch["Killswitch
Lines 19-25"] BaseFlags --> LandOrRemoveModerate["Land or remove (moderate effort)
Lines 26-34"] BaseFlags --> SlatedForRemoval["Slated for removal (significant effort)
Lines 36-61"] BaseFlags --> OngoingExperiments["Ongoing experiments
Lines 62-155"] BaseFlags --> ReadyForMajor["Ready for next major
Lines 156-191"] ``` **Sources:** [packages/shared/ReactFeatureFlags.js:10-191]() ### Key Flag Categories | Category | Purpose | Example Flags | Lines | |----------|---------|---------------|-------| | **Land or remove (moderate)** | Flags requiring migration effort | `disableSchedulerTimeoutInWorkLoop` | [33]() | | **Slated for removal** | Deprecated features awaiting migration | `enableSuspenseCallback`, `enableScopeAPI`, `enableCreateEventHandleAPI` | [50, 53, 56]() | | **Ongoing experiments** | Active feature development | `enableLegacyCache`, `enableTaint`, `enableViewTransition` | [75, 79, 81]() | | **Ready for next major** | Features planned for next major release | `disableLegacyContext`, `disableLegacyMode`, `disableClientCache` | [170, 186, 180]() | **Sources:** [packages/shared/ReactFeatureFlags.js:33-186]() ## Fork Mechanism The fork mechanism allows different builds to use different implementations of the feature flags module. During the build process, Rollup's module resolution is configured to substitute the appropriate fork based on the target bundle type. ### Fork Resolution Process ```mermaid graph TB BuildProcess["Rollup Build Process"] BuildProcess --> BundleType{"Bundle Type"} BundleType -->|"FB_WWW"| WWW["packages/shared/forks/
ReactFeatureFlags.www.js"] BundleType -->|"RN_FB"| NativeFB["packages/shared/forks/
ReactFeatureFlags.native-fb.js"] BundleType -->|"RN_OSS"| NativeOSS["packages/shared/forks/
ReactFeatureFlags.native-oss.js"] BundleType -->|"TEST_RENDERER"| TestRenderer["packages/shared/forks/
ReactFeatureFlags.test-renderer.js"] BundleType -->|"TEST_RENDERER_WWW"| TestRendererWWW["packages/shared/forks/
ReactFeatureFlags.test-renderer.www.js"] BundleType -->|"TEST_RENDERER_NATIVE_FB"| TestRendererNativeFB["packages/shared/forks/
ReactFeatureFlags.test-renderer.native-fb.js"] BundleType -->|"Default"| Base["packages/shared/
ReactFeatureFlags.js"] ``` **Sources:** [packages/shared/forks/ReactFeatureFlags.www.js:1-10](), [packages/shared/forks/ReactFeatureFlags.native-fb.js:1-10](), [packages/shared/forks/ReactFeatureFlags.native-oss.js:1-10]() ### Fork Type Verification Each fork file includes Flow type verification at the end to ensure it exports the same interface as the base file: ```javascript // Flow magic to verify the exports of this file match the original version. ((((null: any): ExportsType): FeatureFlagsType): ExportsType); ``` **Sources:** [packages/shared/forks/ReactFeatureFlags.www.js:126](), [packages/shared/forks/ReactFeatureFlags.native-fb.js:97](), [packages/shared/forks/ReactFeatureFlags.native-oss.js:97]() ## Dynamic Feature Flags Dynamic feature flags enable runtime control of features in internal Meta builds through gatekeeper systems. ### www Dynamic Flags The `ReactFeatureFlags.www.js` fork imports dynamic flags from a runtime module provided by the Facebook infrastructure: ```mermaid graph LR WWWFork["ReactFeatureFlags.www.js"] DynamicModule["ReactFeatureFlags.www-dynamic.js"] GatekeeperSystem["Gatekeeper System
Meta infrastructure"] DynamicModule -->|"require('ReactFeatureFlags')"| GatekeeperSystem GatekeeperSystem --> WWWFork WWWFork -->|"Destructure re-export"| ExportedDynamic["alwaysThrottleRetries
enableObjectFiber
enableViewTransition
enableFragmentRefs"] ``` The www fork destructures and re-exports specific flags from the dynamic module: ```javascript const dynamicFeatureFlags: DynamicFeatureFlags = require('ReactFeatureFlags'); export const { alwaysThrottleRetries, disableLegacyContextForFunctionComponents, disableSchedulerTimeoutInWorkLoop, enableInfiniteRenderLoopDetection, enableNoCloningMemoCache, enableObjectFiber, enableRetryLaneExpiration, enableTransitionTracing, // ... } = dynamicFeatureFlags; ``` **Sources:** [packages/shared/forks/ReactFeatureFlags.www.js:15-37]() ### Native Facebook Dynamic Flags The native-fb fork imports dynamic flags from the `ReactNativeInternalFeatureFlags` native module: ```javascript import * as dynamicFlagsUntyped from 'ReactNativeInternalFeatureFlags'; const dynamicFlags: DynamicExportsType = (dynamicFlagsUntyped: any); export const { alwaysThrottleRetries, enableEffectEventMutationPhase, enableObjectFiber, enableEagerAlternateStateNodeCleanup, passChildrenWhenCloningPersistedNodes, enableFragmentRefs, // ... } = dynamicFlags; ``` **Sources:** [packages/shared/forks/ReactFeatureFlags.native-fb.js:16-32](), [scripts/flow/xplat.js:10-12]() ### __VARIANT__ Testing Pattern Dynamic flag definition files use the `__VARIANT__` macro to enable A/B testing during development. The build system runs tests twice: once with `__VARIANT__ = true` and once with `__VARIANT__ = false`: ```javascript export const alwaysThrottleRetries: boolean = __VARIANT__; export const enableObjectFiber: boolean = __VARIANT__; export const enableRetryLaneExpiration: boolean = __VARIANT__; ``` **Sources:** [packages/shared/forks/ReactFeatureFlags.www-dynamic.js:16-40](), [packages/shared/forks/ReactFeatureFlags.native-fb-dynamic.js:20-29]() ## Build-Time Macros Build-time macros are identifiers replaced with boolean values during the build process to enable conditional compilation. ### __EXPERIMENTAL__ Macro Used to enable features only in experimental release channels: ```javascript export const enableLegacyCache = __EXPERIMENTAL__; export const enableAsyncIterableChildren = __EXPERIMENTAL__; export const enableTaint = __EXPERIMENTAL__; export const enableFizzBlockingRender = __EXPERIMENTAL__; ``` **Sources:** [packages/shared/ReactFeatureFlags.js:75-91]() ### __PROFILE__ Macro Used to enable profiling and performance measurement features: ```javascript export const enableProfilerTimer = __PROFILE__; export const enableProfilerCommitHooks = __PROFILE__; export const enableProfilerNestedUpdatePhase = __PROFILE__; export const enableUpdaterTracking = __PROFILE__; ``` **Sources:** [packages/shared/forks/ReactFeatureFlags.www.js:42-45](), [packages/shared/forks/ReactFeatureFlags.native-fb.js:54-56]() ## Summary of Platform Variations | Flag | Base | www | native-fb | native-oss | |------|------|-----|-----------|------------| | `enableLegacyFBSupport` | false | true | false | false | | `disableLegacyMode` | true | true | false | false | | `enableMoveBefore` | false | false | true | true | | `enableSuspenseCallback` | false | true | true | false | | `enableScopeAPI` | false | true | false | false | | `enableTaint` | __EXPERIMENTAL__ | false | true | true | **Sources:** [packages/shared/ReactFeatureFlags.js:59, 186, 177, 50, 53, 79](), [packages/shared/forks/ReactFeatureFlags.www.js:53, 97, 51, 83, 81, 73](), [packages/shared/forks/ReactFeatureFlags.native-fb.js:51, 40, 47, 62, 60, 63](), [packages/shared/forks/ReactFeatureFlags.native-oss.js:35, 24, 31, 49, 45, 50]() --- # Page: Build System and Package Distribution # Build System and Package Distribution

This page introduces the build infrastructure used to compile, bundle, and distribute React packages. It covers the Rollup-based build pipeline, the bundle type taxonomy, the multi-target output layout, and the multi-channel release model. Details on each sub-topic are continued in child pages: - For the full Rollup pipeline, module forking, and build validation, see [Build Pipeline and Module Forking](#3.1). - For release channel versioning and environment-specific forks, see [Release Channels and Environment Forks](#3.2). --- ## Overview React is a monorepo managed with Yarn Workspaces ([package.json:3-5]()). All source packages live under `packages/`. Building does not happen via a simple `tsc` or Webpack execution; instead, a custom Rollup pipeline in `scripts/rollup/` processes each package into multiple output artifacts suited for different runtime environments (Node, Browser, React Native) and release targets (Stable, Experimental, Meta-internal). The top-level build entry point is: ```bash yarn build # Executes node ./scripts/rollup/build-all-release-channels.js ``` This invokes `scripts/rollup/build.js` once per configured release channel, setting the `RELEASE_CHANNEL` environment variable to control which bundles are included in each output ([scripts/rollup/build-all-release-channels.js:1-15]()). --- ## Toolchain The build system integrates several specialized tools to handle React's complex environment requirements: | Tool | Role | |---|---| | **Rollup** | Primary bundler; processes each bundle entry point defined in `bundles.js` ([scripts/rollup/build.js:3]()). | | **Babel** | Transpiles Flow types, JSX, and language features ([scripts/rollup/build.js:4]()). | | **Google Closure Compiler** | Production minification using `SIMPLE` level optimizations ([scripts/rollup/build.js:5]()). | | **Prettier** | Reformats Closure-compiled output for readability in development artifacts ([scripts/rollup/build.js:8]()). | | **flow-remove-types** | Strips Flow annotations from source files ([scripts/rollup/build.js:6]()). | | **rollup-plugin-dts** | Generates `.d.ts` type declaration bundles for TypeScript consumers ([scripts/rollup/build.js:7]()). | Sources: [package.json:44-107](), [scripts/rollup/build.js:1-30]() --- ## Bundle Type System Every package entry point is compiled into one or more **bundle types**, each representing a distinct target environment and optimization level. Bundle types are declared as constants in `scripts/rollup/bundles.js`. **Bundle Type Hierarchy:** ```mermaid graph TD A["bundleTypes"] --> B["OSS Node / Browser"] A --> C["Meta WWW (internal)"] A --> D["React Native"] A --> E["Type Declarations"] subgraph "OSS_Variants" B --> B1["NODE_DEV"] B --> B2["NODE_PROD"] B --> B3["NODE_PROFILING"] B --> B4["NODE_ES2015"] B --> B5["ESM_DEV"] B --> B6["ESM_PROD"] end subgraph "Meta_WWW" C --> C1["FB_WWW_DEV"] C --> C2["FB_WWW_PROD"] C --> C3["FB_WWW_PROFILING"] end subgraph "Mobile" D --> D1["RN_OSS_DEV"] D --> D2["RN_FB_DEV"] D --> D3["RN_FB_PROD"] end subgraph "Types" E --> E1["CJS_DTS"] E --> E2["ESM_DTS"] end ``` Sources: [scripts/rollup/bundles.js:10-54]() Each bundle type maps to a specific Rollup output format (e.g., `cjs` for Node/RN, `es` for ESM) and determines whether `__DEV__` and `__PROFILE__` flags are enabled ([scripts/rollup/build.js:225-310]()). --- ## Module Types Each bundle also carries a **module type** that describes its architectural role and how it relates to the React Reconciler: | Module Type | Description | Example | |---|---|---| | `ISOMORPHIC` | Core logic that runs in any environment without renderer coupling. | `react`, `react/jsx-runtime` | | `RENDERER` | Packages that bundle the reconciler and couple to a host (DOM, Native). | `react-dom`, `react-native-renderer` | | `RENDERER_UTILS` | Helper packages that access a specific renderer's internals. | `react-dom/test-utils` | | `RECONCILER` | The standalone reconciler for building third-party renderers. | `react-reconciler` | Sources: [scripts/rollup/bundles.js:56-67]() --- ## Build Pipeline Flow The build pipeline transforms source code through a series of plugins and wrappers before writing to the `build/` directory. **Build pipeline for a single bundle variant:** ```mermaid flowchart TD Start["Bundles.bundles entry"] --> Resolve["resolveEntryFork()"] Resolve --> Plugins["getPlugins() Chain"] subgraph "Plugin_Stack" Plugins --> P1["useForks() - Module Substitution"] P1 --> P2["flowRemoveTypes()"] P2 --> P3["babel() - JSX/ES Transpilation"] P3 --> P4["replace() - __DEV__ / Feature Flags"] P4 --> P5["closure() - Minification"] end P5 --> Wrap["Wrappers - License & Module Guards"] Wrap --> Output["getBundleOutputPath()"] Output --> Dir1["build/node_modules/ (OSS)"] Output --> Dir2["build/facebook-www/ (Meta)"] Output --> Dir3["build/facebook-fbsource/ (RN)"] ``` Sources: [scripts/rollup/build.js:354-546](), [scripts/rollup/build.js:636-750](), [scripts/rollup/packaging.js:48-115]() --- ## Module Forking Because the same source file must behave differently in different target environments (e.g., DOM vs. Native), the build system uses a **module forking** mechanism. `scripts/rollup/forks.js` defines a mapping from source file paths to resolver functions that return alternate file paths based on the `bundleType`. **Key Forked Modules:** | Source Path | Purpose of Fork | |---|---| | `shared/ReactFeatureFlags.js` | Substitutes environment-specific feature flag values ([scripts/rollup/forks.js:134-191]()). | | `shared/ReactSharedInternals.js` | Resolves to Client vs. Server internal state holders ([scripts/rollup/forks.js:55-89]()). | | `shared/ReactDOMSharedInternals.js` | Handles FB-specific DOM internals ([scripts/rollup/forks.js:93-131]()). | | `scheduler/src/SchedulerFeatureFlags.js` | Customizes scheduler behavior for WWW vs. OSS ([scripts/rollup/forks.js:193-204]()). | Sources: [scripts/rollup/forks.js:29-484](), [scripts/shared/inlinedHostConfigs.js:9-]() --- ## Output and Distribution The build writes artifacts into the `build/` directory. The layout is optimized for different distribution channels: 1. **`build/node_modules/`**: Contains npm-publishable packages. `packaging.js` handles copying `package.json`, `LICENSE`, and `README.md` files ([scripts/rollup/packaging.js:158-251]()). 2. **`build/facebook-www/`**: Contains specialized bundles for Meta's internal web infrastructure. 3. **`build/facebook-fbsource/`**: Contains bundles for Meta's internal React Native integration. The `exports` map in package files (e.g., `packages/react-dom/package.json`) uses conditional exports to route environments (like `react-server`) to the correct bundle artifact ([packages/react-dom/package.json:51-126]()). --- ## Key Script Commands | Command | Description | |---|---| | `yarn build` | Full multi-channel build via `build-all-release-channels.js` ([package.json:126]()). | | `yarn build-for-devtools` | Builds packages specifically required by React DevTools ([package.json:127]()). | | `yarn lint-build` | Post-build validation of emitted artifacts ([package.json:135]()). | | `yarn version-check` | Verifies version consistency across the monorepo ([package.json:149]()). | Sources: [package.json:124-158]() --- # Page: Build Pipeline and Module Forking # Build Pipeline and Module Forking

## Purpose and Scope This page documents React's build pipeline architecture, focusing on how source code is transformed into multiple environment-specific bundles. It covers the bundle type system defined in `bundles.js`, the module forking mechanism in `forks.js`, entry point resolution, and the Rollup-based transformation pipeline. For information about release channels and feature flags integration with the build system, see [3.2 Release Channels and Environment Forks](). For the overall build system architecture, see [3. Build System and Package Distribution](). ## Bundle Type System React's build system produces bundles for many different environments and use cases. Each bundle is categorized by a `bundleType` that determines its build configuration and target platform. ### Bundle Types The `bundleTypes` object in [scripts/rollup/bundles.js:10-31]() defines all supported bundle types: | Bundle Type | Purpose | Output Format | |------------|---------|---------------| | `NODE_ES2015` | Node.js ES2015+ modules | CommonJS | | `NODE_DEV` / `NODE_PROD` | Node.js development/production | CommonJS | | `NODE_PROFILING` | Node.js with profiling enabled | CommonJS | | `ESM_DEV` / `ESM_PROD` | ECMAScript modules | ES Modules | | `BUN_DEV` / `BUN_PROD` | Bun runtime bundles | CommonJS | | `FB_WWW_DEV` / `FB_WWW_PROD` / `FB_WWW_PROFILING` | Facebook.com website | CommonJS | | `RN_OSS_DEV` / `RN_OSS_PROD` / `RN_OSS_PROFILING` | React Native open source | CommonJS | | `RN_FB_DEV` / `RN_FB_PROD` / `RN_FB_PROFILING` | React Native Facebook internal | CommonJS | | `BROWSER_SCRIPT` | Standalone browser scripts | IIFE | | `CJS_DTS` / `ESM_DTS` | TypeScript type definitions | Type definitions | ### Module Types The `moduleTypes` object [scripts/rollup/bundles.js:56-65]() categorizes packages by their architectural role: - `ISOMORPHIC`: Packages that work in any environment (e.g., `react`). - `RENDERER`: Packages that implement platform-specific rendering (e.g., `react-dom`, `react-native-renderer`). - `RENDERER_UTILS`: Helper packages for specific renderers (e.g., `react-dom/test-utils`). - `RECONCILER`: The standalone reconciler package (`react-reconciler`). **Sources:** [scripts/rollup/bundles.js:10-67]() ## Bundle Definitions ### Bundle Configuration Structure Each bundle in [scripts/rollup/bundles.js:69-1175]() is defined as an object with properties that drive the Rollup configuration. ```javascript { bundleTypes: [NODE_DEV, NODE_PROD, FB_WWW_DEV, ...], moduleType: ISOMORPHIC, entry: 'react', global: 'React', minifyWithProdErrorCodes: false, wrapWithModuleBoundaries: true, externals: ['ReactNativeInternalFeatureFlags'] } ``` **Key Properties:** - `bundleTypes`: Array of bundle types to build [scripts/rollup/bundles.js:72-81](). - `moduleType`: Architectural classification [scripts/rollup/bundles.js:82](). - `entry`: Entry point module path [scripts/rollup/bundles.js:83](). - `global`: Global variable name for UMD/IIFE bundles [scripts/rollup/bundles.js:84](). - `minifyWithProdErrorCodes`: Whether to replace error messages with codes [scripts/rollup/bundles.js:85](). - `wrapWithModuleBoundaries`: Whether to wrap with DevTools module boundaries [scripts/rollup/bundles.js:86](). - `externals`: Dependencies to treat as external [scripts/rollup/bundles.js:87](). ### Bundle Definition Logic ```mermaid graph TD subgraph "Code Entity Space: bundles.js" BundleDef["bundles[] Array"] BundleObj["Bundle Object Entry"] end subgraph "Natural Language Space: Build Targets" Entry["Entry: 'react-dom'"] Types["Types: NODE_DEV, NODE_PROD"] Module["Module: RENDERER"] end BundleDef --> BundleObj BundleObj --> Entry BundleObj --> Types BundleObj --> Module Entry --> Resolution["resolveEntryFork() in build.js"] Types --> Pipeline["getPlugins() in build.js"] Module --> Forks["getForks() in forks.js"] Resolution --> Output1["build/node_modules/react-dom/cjs/react-dom.development.js"] Resolution --> Output2["build/node_modules/react-dom/cjs/react-dom.production.js"] ``` **Sources:** [scripts/rollup/bundles.js:69-1175](), [scripts/rollup/build.js:18-28]() ## Module Forking System The forking system allows React to use different implementations of the same module for different environments (e.g., `www` vs `native`) without changing import statements in the source code. ### Fork Resolution Process The `forks` object in [scripts/rollup/forks.js:52-482]() maps canonical module paths to resolver functions. These functions receive `bundleType`, `entry`, and `dependencies` to determine the correct file to use. ```mermaid graph TD subgraph "Code Entity Space: forks.js" ForksMap["forks Object Map"] ResolveFn["Resolver Function"] FindFork["findNearestExistingForkFile()"] end subgraph "Natural Language Space: Module Redirection" Import["import from 'shared/ReactFeatureFlags'"] CheckEnv{"Check Environment?"} WWW["Use .www.js"] Native["Use .native-fb.js"] Default["Use base .js"] end Import --> ForksMap ForksMap --> ResolveFn ResolveFn --> CheckEnv CheckEnv -->|FB_WWW| WWW CheckEnv -->|RN_FB| Native CheckEnv -->|Other| Default WWW --> FindFork Native --> FindFork ``` ### Core Fork Definitions - **ReactFeatureFlags Fork** [scripts/rollup/forks.js:134-191](): Redirects to `ReactFeatureFlags.www.js`, `ReactFeatureFlags.native-fb.js`, or `ReactFeatureFlags.native-oss.js` based on the `bundleType`. - **SchedulerFeatureFlags Fork** [scripts/rollup/forks.js:193-207](): Redirects scheduler flags for `www` environments. - **ReactFiberConfig Fork** [scripts/rollup/forks.js:242-274](): Dynamically resolves renderer configurations (e.g., `dom-browser`) using `findNearestExistingForkFile`. **Sources:** [scripts/rollup/forks.js:29-482](), [scripts/shared/inlinedHostConfigs.js:1-518]() ## Entry Point Resolution React uses file naming conventions to provide different implementations for different release channels and environments. The `resolveEntryFork` function in [scripts/rollup/build.js:585-633]() implements this logic. ### Resolution Precedence For Facebook bundles (`isFBBundle`): 1. `*.modern.fb.js` (if `__EXPERIMENTAL__` is true) [scripts/rollup/build.js:591-595]() 2. `*.classic.fb.js` (if `__EXPERIMENTAL__` is false) [scripts/rollup/build.js:596-600]() 3. `*.fb.js` [scripts/rollup/build.js:601-605]() For Open Source bundles: 1. `*.experimental.js` (if `__EXPERIMENTAL__` is true) [scripts/rollup/build.js:616-620]() 2. `*.stable.js` (if `__EXPERIMENTAL__` is false) [scripts/rollup/build.js:621-625]() 3. `*.js` (base file) [scripts/rollup/build.js:626-628]() **Sources:** [scripts/rollup/build.js:585-633]() ## Build Plugin Pipeline The build process uses Rollup with a series of plugins that transform source code from Flow/TypeScript into environment-specific JavaScript. ### Plugin Pipeline Architecture | Plugin | Role | Implementation | |--------|------|----------------| | `dynamic-imports` | Handles code splitting boundaries | [scripts/rollup/build.js:23]() | | `use-forks-plugin` | Performs the module redirection defined in `forks.js` | [scripts/rollup/build.js:22]() | | `babel` | Transforms ESNext, Flow, and React JSX | [scripts/rollup/build.js:4]() | | `closure-plugin` | Minification and advanced optimizations | [scripts/rollup/build.js:5]() | | `replace` | Injects `__DEV__` and `__EXPERIMENTAL__` constants | [scripts/rollup/build.js:9]() | | `strip-banner` | Removes license headers for internal processing | [scripts/rollup/build.js:11]() | ### Babel Configuration The `getBabelConfig` function [scripts/rollup/build.js:143-172]() configures transformations. It includes: - **Error Code Transformation**: Replaces error messages with unique IDs in production [scripts/rollup/build.js:167-169](). - **ES5 Transforms**: Applied only to development builds to ensure compatibility [scripts/rollup/build.js:159-161](). - **Object.assign Transform**: Replaces object spread with a shared helper [scripts/rollup/build.js:129-130](). **Sources:** [scripts/rollup/build.js:111-172](), [scripts/rollup/plugins/closure-plugin.js]() ## Code Wrapping System Different bundle types require different wrappers to integrate with their target environments. This is handled by `wrappers.js`. ### Wrapper Types - **Development Wrapper** [scripts/rollup/wrappers.js:86-95](): Wraps code in a `process.env.NODE_ENV !== "production"` check and an IIFE. - **FB_WWW Development Wrapper** [scripts/rollup/wrappers.js:107-116](): Wraps code in an `if (__DEV__)` check. - **Reconciler Wrapper** [scripts/rollup/wrappers.js:189-198](): Exports a function named `$$$reconciler` that accepts a configuration object. - **License Header** [scripts/rollup/wrappers.js:12-25](): Appends the standard React license and version information to the top of every bundle. **Sources:** [scripts/rollup/wrappers.js:1-393](), [scripts/rollup/build.js:446-456]() ## Output Path Resolution The `getBundleOutputPath` function in [scripts/rollup/packaging.js:48-115]() determines where each bundle is written based on its `bundleType`. - **Node.js Bundles**: Written to `build/node_modules/{package}/cjs/` [scripts/rollup/packaging.js:57-61](). - **Facebook WWW**: Written to `build/facebook-www/` [scripts/rollup/packaging.js:75-77](). - **React Native**: Written to `build/react-native/implementations/` [scripts/rollup/packaging.js:84-86](). **Sources:** [scripts/rollup/packaging.js:48-115](), [scripts/rollup/build.js:73]() --- # Page: Release Channels and Environment Forks # Release Channels and Environment Forks

## Overview React's build system supports multiple deployment targets from a single codebase through two orthogonal mechanisms: **release channels** and **environment forks**. - **Release channels** (`experimental` vs `stable`) control which experimental features are included in builds via the `RELEASE_CHANNEL` environment variable. - **Environment forks** target specific platforms (Facebook web, React Native Facebook, React Native OSS, standard Node.js) by selecting different bundle types and module implementations. These mechanisms work together with the feature flag system to enable precise control over what code runs in each deployment scenario. The build pipeline processes these configurations to produce environment-specific artifacts. --- ## Release Channel System ### The RELEASE_CHANNEL Environment Variable React uses the `RELEASE_CHANNEL` environment variable to control which experimental features are included in builds. This variable is read at build time and converted to the `__EXPERIMENTAL__` constant that gets inlined throughout the source code. **Core Implementation** [scripts/rollup/bundles.js:3-9]() and [scripts/rollup/build.js:31-38](): ```javascript const RELEASE_CHANNEL = process.env.RELEASE_CHANNEL; const __EXPERIMENTAL__ = typeof RELEASE_CHANNEL === 'string' ? RELEASE_CHANNEL === 'experimental' : true; ``` ### Channel Values | RELEASE_CHANNEL Value | __EXPERIMENTAL__ | Usage | |----------------------|------------------|-------| | `'experimental'` | `true` | Canary releases, bleeding-edge features enabled | | `'stable'` | `false` | Stable releases, only production-ready features | | `undefined` (default) | `true` | Local development, defaults to experimental | **Key behavior**: When `RELEASE_CHANNEL` is not set, `__EXPERIMENTAL__` defaults to `true`, making experimental mode the default for local development [scripts/rollup/bundles.js:8-9](). ### How __EXPERIMENTAL__ Propagates Through the Build **Constant Replacement Flow** ```mermaid graph TB EnvVar["process.env.RELEASE_CHANNEL"] CheckType{"typeof === 'string'?"} CheckValue{"value === 'experimental'?"} DefaultTrue["__EXPERIMENTAL__ = true"] SetTrue["__EXPERIMENTAL__ = true"] SetFalse["__EXPERIMENTAL__ = false"] EnvVar --> CheckType CheckType -->|"Yes"| CheckValue CheckType -->|"No (undefined)"| DefaultTrue CheckValue -->|"Yes"| SetTrue CheckValue -->|"No"| SetFalse DefaultTrue --> RollupReplace["@rollup/plugin-replace"] SetTrue --> RollupReplace SetFalse --> RollupReplace RollupReplace --> InlineValue["Inline __EXPERIMENTAL__ → true/false"] InlineValue --> EntryForks["Select .experimental.js vs .stable.js"] InlineValue --> FlagValues["Set flag default values"] InlineValue --> DeadCode["Dead code elimination"] ``` Sources: [scripts/rollup/bundles.js:3-9](), [scripts/rollup/build.js:31-38](). --- ## Environment-Specific Bundle Types React targets multiple deployment environments through **bundle types**. Each bundle type represents a specific combination of target platform, module format, and optimization level. ### Bundle Type Definitions [scripts/rollup/bundles.js:10-31]() defines all supported bundle types: ```javascript const bundleTypes = { NODE_ES2015: 'NODE_ES2015', ESM_DEV: 'ESM_DEV', ESM_PROD: 'ESM_PROD', NODE_DEV: 'NODE_DEV', NODE_PROD: 'NODE_PROD', NODE_PROFILING: 'NODE_PROFILING', BUN_DEV: 'BUN_DEV', BUN_PROD: 'BUN_PROD', FB_WWW_DEV: 'FB_WWW_DEV', FB_WWW_PROD: 'FB_WWW_PROD', FB_WWW_PROFILING: 'FB_WWW_PROFILING', RN_OSS_DEV: 'RN_OSS_DEV', RN_OSS_PROD: 'RN_OSS_PROD', RN_OSS_PROFILING: 'RN_OSS_PROFILING', RN_FB_DEV: 'RN_FB_DEV', RN_FB_PROD: 'RN_FB_PROD', RN_FB_PROFILING: 'RN_FB_PROFILING', BROWSER_SCRIPT: 'BROWSER_SCRIPT', CJS_DTS: 'CJS_DTS', ESM_DTS: 'ESM_DTS', }; ``` ### Environment Categories | Environment | Bundle Types | Purpose | |-------------|--------------|---------| | **Standard Node.js** | `NODE_DEV`, `NODE_PROD`, `NODE_PROFILING`, `NODE_ES2015` | Open source npm packages for Node.js | | **ESM/Browser** | `ESM_DEV`, `ESM_PROD`, `BROWSER_SCRIPT` | ES modules and browser scripts | | **Bun Runtime** | `BUN_DEV`, `BUN_PROD` | Bun-specific optimizations | | **Facebook WWW** | `FB_WWW_DEV`, `FB_WWW_PROD`, `FB_WWW_PROFILING` | Facebook's internal web infrastructure | | **React Native OSS** | `RN_OSS_DEV`, `RN_OSS_PROD`, `RN_OSS_PROFILING` | Open source React Native | | **React Native FB** | `RN_FB_DEV`, `RN_FB_PROD`, `RN_FB_PROFILING` | Facebook's internal React Native | Sources: [scripts/rollup/bundles.js:10-54](). --- ## Environment Fork System React uses file naming conventions and a plugin-based resolution system to provide different implementations for different environments. ### Module Forking Implementation The `use-forks-plugin` is a core part of the Rollup pipeline [scripts/rollup/build.js:22](). It uses a mapping defined in `forks.js` to swap module paths based on the current `bundleType` and `entry` point. **Decision Tree for Feature Flags** The most common use of forking is for `ReactFeatureFlags.js`. The logic in [scripts/rollup/forks.js:134-191]() determines which implementation is used: ```mermaid graph TD Entry["Entry Point Name"] BundleType["Bundle Type"] Entry -->|react-native-renderer| RNBundles{Bundle Type?} RNBundles -->|RN_FB_*| RNFBFlags["ReactFeatureFlags.native-fb.js"] RNBundles -->|RN_OSS_*| RNOSSFlags["ReactFeatureFlags.native-oss.js"] Entry -->|react-test-renderer| TestBundles{Bundle Type?} TestBundles -->|RN_FB_*| TestRNFB["ReactFeatureFlags.test-renderer.native-fb.js"] TestBundles -->|FB_WWW_*| TestWWW["ReactFeatureFlags.test-renderer.www.js"] TestBundles -->|Other| TestStd["ReactFeatureFlags.test-renderer.js"] Entry -->|Other| DefaultBundles{Bundle Type?} DefaultBundles -->|FB_WWW_*| WWWFlags["ReactFeatureFlags.www.js"] DefaultBundles -->|RN_FB_*| RNFBFlags DefaultBundles -->|Other| Canonical["ReactFeatureFlags.js (Base)"] ``` ### Internal Shared Internals Forking Forks are also used to resolve cyclical dependencies or provide environment-specific shared state. For example, `ReactSharedInternals.js` is forked based on whether the entry point is the `react` package itself or a server-side entry [scripts/rollup/forks.js:55-89](). Sources: [scripts/rollup/forks.js:1-191](), [scripts/rollup/build.js:22](). --- ## Interaction with Feature Flags Release channels directly influence which feature flag configurations are loaded. The fork resolution system selects flag files based on both the bundle type and release channel. ### Channel-Dependent Flag Values Many flags in the canonical `ReactFeatureFlags.js` use `__EXPERIMENTAL__` as their default value, making them channel-dependent [packages/shared/ReactFeatureFlags.js:75-112](): ```javascript // From packages/shared/ReactFeatureFlags.js export const enableLegacyCache = __EXPERIMENTAL__; export const enableAsyncIterableChildren = __EXPERIMENTAL__; export const enableTaint = __EXPERIMENTAL__; export const enableGestureTransition = __EXPERIMENTAL__; export const enableFizzBlockingRender = __EXPERIMENTAL__; ``` ### Dynamic Flags and __VARIANT__ Facebook environments (WWW and RN_FB) support **dynamic flags** for A/B testing. These use the `__VARIANT__` constant, which creates two test variants in the testing infrastructure. **Example from** [packages/shared/forks/ReactFeatureFlags.www-dynamic.js:1-47](): ```javascript // In www, these flags are controlled by GKs (gatekeeper/feature flags) export const alwaysThrottleRetries: boolean = __VARIANT__; export const disableLegacyContextForFunctionComponents: boolean = __VARIANT__; export const enableObjectFiber: boolean = __VARIANT__; ``` Sources: [packages/shared/ReactFeatureFlags.js:75-112](), [packages/shared/forks/ReactFeatureFlags.www-dynamic.js:1-47](). --- ## Build Output Organization Build artifacts are organized by environment, with different directory structures for different deployment targets. ### Output Path Resolution The `getBundleOutputPath()` function in [scripts/rollup/packaging.js:48-115]() determines where each bundle is written: | Bundle Type | Output Path Pattern | |-------------|-------------------| | `NODE_*` | `build/node_modules/{package}/cjs/{filename}` | | `ESM_*` | `build/node_modules/{package}/esm/{filename}` | | `FB_WWW_*` | `build/facebook-www/{filename}` | | `RN_OSS_*` | `build/react-native/implementations/{filename}` | | `RN_FB_*` | `build/facebook-react-native/{package}/cjs/{filename}` | ### Package Preparation Post-build, the `prepareNpmPackages` function [scripts/rollup/packaging.js:253-284]() cleans up the artifacts. It uses `filterOutEntrypoints` [scripts/rollup/packaging.js:171-251]() to remove entry points from `package.json` that are not intended for the current release channel or environment (e.g., internal Facebook-only entry points). Sources: [scripts/rollup/packaging.js:48-115](), [scripts/rollup/packaging.js:171-284](). --- # Page: React Reconciler # React Reconciler

## Purpose and Scope The React Reconciler is the core rendering engine that coordinates the process of updating the user interface in response to state changes. It implements the Fiber architecture, manages work scheduling through a priority-based lane system, and orchestrates the three-phase rendering process: reconciliation (`beginWork`), instance creation (`completeWork`), and effects application (`commitWork`). [packages/react-reconciler/src/ReactFiberWorkLoop.js:232-235](). This document provides a high-level introduction to the reconciler's architecture and responsibilities. For detailed information about specific subsystems, see: - Fiber data structures and properties: [Fiber Architecture and Data Structures](#4.1) - Rendering phases and work loop: [Work Loop and Rendering Phases](#4.2) - Hooks implementation: [React Hooks System](#4.3) - Priority scheduling: [Lane-Based Scheduling and Priorities](#4.4) - Error handling and deferred rendering: [Suspense, Error Boundaries, and Offscreen](#4.5) - Performance measurement: [Profiling and Performance Tracking](#4.6) - Platform-specific implementations: [Platform Renderers](#6) Sources: [packages/react-reconciler/src/ReactFiberReconciler.js:10-130](), [packages/react-reconciler/src/ReactFiberWorkLoop.js:1-100]() ## Core Responsibilities The reconciler is platform-agnostic and performs the following key functions: 1. **Virtual Tree Management**: Maintains two Fiber trees (`current` and `workInProgress`) using a double-buffering technique. [packages/react-reconciler/src/ReactFiber.js:327-394]() 2. **Work Scheduling**: Prioritizes updates using a lane-based system and schedules work with the concurrent scheduler. [packages/react-reconciler/src/ReactFiberLane.js:180-221]() 3. **Reconciliation**: Compares new elements against the current tree to determine minimal changes. [packages/react-reconciler/src/ReactChildFiber.js:1240-1280]() 4. **Instance Management**: Creates and updates host instances through platform-specific `HostConfig` interfaces. [packages/react-reconciler/src/ReactFiberCompleteWork.js:106-130]() 5. **Effects Coordination**: Manages side effects including refs, layout effects, and passive effects. [packages/react-reconciler/src/ReactFiberCommitWork.js:344-473]() 6. **State Management**: Coordinates component state, hooks, and context propagation. [packages/react-reconciler/src/ReactFiberClassComponent.js:165-212]() Sources: [packages/react-reconciler/src/ReactFiberWorkLoop.js:1-100](), [packages/react-reconciler/src/ReactFiberReconciler.js:1-130]() ## Fiber Architecture and Double Buffering ### The Fiber Data Structure Every React element is represented by a `Fiber` object, which serves as a unit of work. The Fiber contains metadata about the component, its state, and its relationship to other fibers in the tree. [packages/react-reconciler/src/ReactFiber.js:138-211](). ```mermaid graph TB FiberNode["FiberNode (ReactFiber.js)"] Instance["Instance Fields"] Tree["Tree Structure"] State["State & Props"] Effects["Effects & Flags"] Scheduling["Scheduling"] FiberNode --> Instance FiberNode --> Tree FiberNode --> State FiberNode --> Effects FiberNode --> Scheduling Instance --> tag["tag: WorkTag (ReactWorkTags.js)"] Instance --> type["type: any"] Instance --> stateNode["stateNode: any"] Instance --> elementType["elementType: any"] Tree --> return["return: Fiber | null"] Tree --> child["child: Fiber | null"] Tree --> sibling["sibling: Fiber | null"] Tree --> alternate["alternate: Fiber | null"] State --> pendingProps["pendingProps: any"] State --> memoizedProps["memoizedProps: any"] State --> memoizedState["memoizedState: any"] State --> updateQueue["updateQueue: mixed"] Effects --> flags["flags: Flags (ReactFiberFlags.js)"] Effects --> subtreeFlags["subtreeFlags: Flags"] Effects --> deletions["deletions: Array | null"] Scheduling --> lanes["lanes: Lanes (ReactFiberLane.js)"] Scheduling --> childLanes["childLanes: Lanes"] ``` **Fiber Tree Structure** Each Fiber maintains pointers to form a linked tree structure: - `child`: First child fiber. - `sibling`: Next sibling fiber. - `return`: Parent fiber. - `alternate`: Corresponding fiber in the other tree (current ↔ workInProgress). [packages/react-reconciler/src/ReactFiber.js:153-178](). Sources: [packages/react-reconciler/src/ReactFiber.js:138-211](), [packages/react-reconciler/src/ReactWorkTags.js:49-78]() ### Double Buffering Technique The reconciler maintains two Fiber trees to enable non-blocking updates. The `FiberRootNode` points to the `current` tree, while the work loop operates on the `workInProgress` tree. [packages/react-reconciler/src/ReactFiberWorkLoop.js:129-130](). ```mermaid graph LR subgraph "FiberRootNode (ReactFiberRoot.js)" RootNode["FiberRootNode"] end subgraph "Current Tree" C_Host["current: HostRoot Fiber"] C_App["App Fiber"] C_Div["div Fiber"] end subgraph "Work-in-Progress Tree" W_Host["workInProgress: HostRoot Fiber"] W_App["App Fiber (clone)"] W_Div["div Fiber (clone)"] end subgraph "Host Environment" DOM["DOM Tree (Instance)"] end RootNode -.->|current| C_Host C_Host --> C_App C_App --> C_Div W_Host --> W_App W_App --> W_Div C_Host -.->|alternate| W_Host C_App -.->|alternate| W_App C_Div -.->|alternate| W_Div C_Div -.->|stateNode| DOM W_Div -.->|stateNode| DOM ``` **Double Buffering Flow:** 1. Updates begin work on the `workInProgress` tree via `createWorkInProgress()`. [packages/react-reconciler/src/ReactFiber.js:327-330]() 2. Work proceeds on `workInProgress` without disrupting the `current` tree. 3. When work completes, the `current` pointer on the root is swapped to the `workInProgress` tree during the commit phase. [packages/react-reconciler/src/ReactFiberWorkLoop.js:2040-2050]() 4. The old `current` tree becomes the new `workInProgress` tree for the next update. Sources: [packages/react-reconciler/src/ReactFiber.js:327-394](), [packages/react-reconciler/src/ReactFiberWorkLoop.js:129-130]() ## Three-Phase Rendering Pipeline The reconciler processes updates through three distinct phases executed by the work loop: ```mermaid graph TD ScheduleUpdate["scheduleUpdateOnFiber() (ReactFiberWorkLoop.js)"] EnsureScheduled["ensureRootIsScheduled()"] subgraph "Render Phase (Interruptible)" BeginWork["beginWork() (ReactFiberBeginWork.js)"] CompleteWork["completeWork() (ReactFiberCompleteWork.js)"] UnwindWork["unwindWork() (ReactFiberUnwindWork.js)"] end subgraph "Commit Phase (Synchronous)" BeforeMutation["commitBeforeMutationEffects() (ReactFiberCommitWork.js)"] Mutation["commitMutationEffects()"] Layout["commitLayoutEffects()"] Passive["commitPassiveEffects() (scheduled)"] end ScheduleUpdate --> EnsureScheduled EnsureScheduled -->|performConcurrentWorkOnRoot| BeginWork BeginWork -->|traverse down| BeginWork BeginWork -->|child complete| CompleteWork BeginWork -->|suspense/error| UnwindWork CompleteWork -->|traverse up| CompleteWork CompleteWork -->|sibling exists| BeginWork CompleteWork -->|root complete| BeforeMutation UnwindWork -->|boundary found| BeginWork BeforeMutation --> Mutation Mutation --> Layout Layout --> Passive ``` ### Phase 1: Render Phase (BeginWork + CompleteWork) **BeginWork Phase** (`ReactFiberBeginWork.js`): - Traverses down the tree starting from the root. - Reconciles children by comparing new elements with existing fibers. [packages/react-reconciler/src/ReactFiberBeginWork.js:141-145]() - Determines if work can be skipped via bailout optimizations. [packages/react-reconciler/src/ReactFiberBeginWork.js:225-226]() **CompleteWork Phase** (`ReactFiberCompleteWork.js`): - Traverses up the tree after reaching a leaf. - Creates or updates host instances (e.g., DOM nodes) using `createInstance`. [packages/react-reconciler/src/ReactFiberCompleteWork.js:106-110]() - Bubbles flags up to parent fibers. Sources: [packages/react-reconciler/src/ReactFiberBeginWork.js:232-234](), [packages/react-reconciler/src/ReactFiberCompleteWork.js:235-240]() ### Phase 2: Commit Phase (CommitWork) The commit phase is synchronous and applies all changes to the host environment. It is orchestrated in `ReactFiberCommitWork.js`. 1. **Before Mutation**: Calls `getSnapshotBeforeUpdate` and handles focus. [packages/react-reconciler/src/ReactFiberCommitWork.js:244-245]() 2. **Mutation**: Applies DOM mutations like `appendChild` or `commitUpdate`. [packages/react-reconciler/src/ReactFiberCommitWork.js:248-250]() 3. **Layout**: Fires `useLayoutEffect` and `componentDidMount`. [packages/react-reconciler/src/ReactFiberCommitWork.js:247-247]() 4. **Passive**: Scheduled asynchronously to fire `useEffect`. [packages/react-reconciler/src/ReactFiberCommitWork.js:249-250]() Sources: [packages/react-reconciler/src/ReactFiberCommitWork.js:93-121](), [packages/react-reconciler/src/ReactFiberCommitEffects.js:221-238]() ## Coordination with Renderers The reconciler communicates with platform-specific renderers through the `HostConfig` interface. [packages/react-reconciler/src/ReactFiberConfig.js:1-35](). | Method | Phase | Purpose | |--------|-------|---------| | `createInstance` | CompleteWork | Create a host instance (e.g., DOM element) | | `appendChild` | CompleteWork | Append a child during initial render | | `commitUpdate` | Mutation | Apply property updates to a host instance | | `prepareForCommit` | Before Mutation | Prepare the host environment for changes | Sources: [packages/react-reconciler/src/ReactFiberCompleteWork.js:106-130](), [packages/react-reconciler/src/ReactFiberCommitWork.js:156-186]() ## Key Subsystems ### Hooks System Manages state and side effects in function components via a linked list on `fiber.memoizedState`. [packages/react-reconciler/src/ReactFiberHooks.js:1-50](). For details, see [React Hooks System](#4.3). ### Lane-Based Priority System Uses bitmasks to represent update priorities, enabling concurrent rendering and interruption. [packages/react-reconciler/src/ReactFiberLane.js:180-221](). For details, see [Lane-Based Scheduling and Priorities](#4.4). ### Suspense and Error Boundaries Handles asynchronous dependencies and runtime errors through the `unwindWork` mechanism. [packages/react-reconciler/src/ReactFiberThrow.js:238-242](). For details, see [Suspense, Error Boundaries, and Offscreen](#4.5). ### Profiler and Performance Tracking Tracks render durations and component performance via `ReactProfilerTimer`. [packages/react-reconciler/src/ReactFiberPerformanceTrack.js:221-227](). For details, see [Profiling and Performance Tracking](#4.6). Sources: [packages/react-reconciler/src/ReactFiberWorkLoop.js:45-60](), [packages/react-reconciler/src/ReactFiberLane.js:1-50]() --- # Page: Fiber Architecture and Data Structures # Fiber Architecture and Data Structures

## Purpose and Scope This page documents the Fiber data structure, its properties (`memoizedState`, `memoizedProps`, `flags`, `lanes`, `alternate`), the Fiber tree structure using `child`/`sibling`/`return` pointers, and how Fibers represent different component types via work tags. These are the foundational data structures that enable React's incremental reconciliation. [packages/react-reconciler/src/ReactFiber.js:138-211]() For related topics, see: - Work loop execution and render/commit phases: [Work Loop and Rendering Phases](4.2) - Hooks implementation and state storage: [React Hooks System](4.3) - Lane-based priority scheduling: [Lane-Based Scheduling and Priorities](4.4) - Suspense boundaries and error handling: [Suspense, Error Boundaries, and Offscreen](4.5) - Performance profiling: [Profiling and Performance Tracking](4.6) ## FiberRoot: The Container The `FiberRoot` is the container object that holds the entire fiber tree and coordinates rendering. It is created by `createFiberRoot` in `ReactFiberRoot.js`. [packages/react-reconciler/src/ReactFiberRoot.js:109-225]() ### FiberRoot Structure Key fields defined in `ReactInternalTypes.js`: | Field | Type | Purpose | |-------|------|---------| | `containerInfo` | `Container` | The host container (e.g., DOM node) [packages/react-reconciler/src/ReactInternalTypes.js:256-256]() | | `current` | `Fiber` | Pointer to the current HostRoot fiber [packages/react-reconciler/src/ReactInternalTypes.js:259-259]() | | `finishedWork` | `Fiber \| null` | Work-in-progress root after render completes [packages/react-reconciler/src/ReactInternalTypes.js:275-275]() | | `pendingLanes` | `Lanes` | Lanes with pending work [packages/react-reconciler/src/ReactInternalTypes.js:283-283]() | | `suspendedLanes` | `Lanes` | Lanes suspended by promises [packages/react-reconciler/src/ReactInternalTypes.js:284-284]() | | `pingedLanes` | `Lanes` | Lanes that have been pinged to retry [packages/react-reconciler/src/ReactInternalTypes.js:285-285]() | | `expiredLanes` | `Lanes` | Lanes that have expired [packages/react-reconciler/src/ReactInternalTypes.js:286-286]() | | `callbackNode` | `mixed` | Scheduler callback node [packages/react-reconciler/src/ReactInternalTypes.js:293-293]() | | `callbackPriority` | `Lane` | Priority of scheduled callback [packages/react-reconciler/src/ReactInternalTypes.js:294-294]() | **Diagram: FiberRoot and HostRoot Relationship** ```mermaid graph TB ContainerDiv["containerInfo
(DOM div)"] FiberRootNode["FiberRoot"] CurrentHostRoot["current
(HostRoot fiber)"] WIPHostRoot["finishedWork
(HostRoot fiber WIP)"] FiberRootNode -->|"containerInfo"| ContainerDiv FiberRootNode -->|"current"| CurrentHostRoot FiberRootNode -->|"finishedWork"| WIPHostRoot CurrentHostRoot -->|"stateNode"| FiberRootNode WIPHostRoot -->|"stateNode"| FiberRootNode CurrentHostRoot -.->|"alternate"| WIPHostRoot WIPHostRoot -.->|"alternate"| CurrentHostRoot CurrentHostRoot -->|"child"| CurrentApp["App fiber (current)"] WIPHostRoot -->|"child"| WIPApp["App fiber (WIP)"] ``` Sources: [packages/react-reconciler/src/ReactFiberRoot.js:109-225](), [packages/react-reconciler/src/ReactInternalTypes.js:253-334]() ## The Fiber Data Structure ### Fiber Node Definition A Fiber is React's internal unit of work. The `FiberNode` constructor is defined in `ReactFiber.js`. [packages/react-reconciler/src/ReactFiber.js:138-211]() ### Fiber Fields by Category | Field Category | Key Fields | Purpose | |---------------|------------|---------| | **Instance Identity** | `tag`, `key`, `type` | Identify what this fiber represents [packages/react-reconciler/src/ReactFiber.js:146-149]() | | **Instance State** | `stateNode` | Reference to component instance or DOM node [packages/react-reconciler/src/ReactFiber.js:150-150]() | | **Tree Structure** | `return`, `child`, `sibling` | Form the fiber tree via pointers [packages/react-reconciler/src/ReactFiber.js:153-155]() | | **Props** | `pendingProps`, `memoizedProps` | Props being processed vs last rendered [packages/react-reconciler/src/ReactFiber.js:161-162]() | | **State** | `memoizedState`, `updateQueue` | Component state and pending updates [packages/react-reconciler/src/ReactFiber.js:163-164]() | | **Effects** | `flags`, `subtreeFlags` | Side-effects to perform in commit phase [packages/react-reconciler/src/ReactFiber.js:170-171]() | | **Scheduling** | `lanes`, `childLanes` | Work priority for fiber and subtree [packages/react-reconciler/src/ReactFiber.js:174-175]() | | **Double Buffering** | `alternate` | Link to counterpart fiber in other tree [packages/react-reconciler/src/ReactFiber.js:177-177]() | Sources: [packages/react-reconciler/src/ReactFiber.js:138-211](), [packages/react-reconciler/src/ReactInternalTypes.js:89-205]() ### Work Tags Each fiber's `tag` identifies its type, defined in `ReactWorkTags.js`. [packages/react-reconciler/src/ReactWorkTags.js:49-78]() | Tag Constant | Value | Represents | |--------------|-------|------------| | `FunctionComponent` | 0 | Function component [packages/react-reconciler/src/ReactWorkTags.js:49-49]() | | `ClassComponent` | 1 | Class component [packages/react-reconciler/src/ReactWorkTags.js:50-50]() | | `HostRoot` | 3 | Root of a fiber tree [packages/react-reconciler/src/ReactWorkTags.js:51-51]() | | `HostComponent` | 5 | Platform element (e.g. `
`) [packages/react-reconciler/src/ReactWorkTags.js:52-52]() | | `SuspenseComponent` | 13 | `` boundary [packages/react-reconciler/src/ReactWorkTags.js:63-63]() | | `OffscreenComponent` | 22 | Hidden/deferred subtree [packages/react-reconciler/src/ReactWorkTags.js:71-71]() | Sources: [packages/react-reconciler/src/ReactWorkTags.js:49-78]() ## Double Buffering: Current and Work-in-Progress Trees React maintains two fiber trees using a technique called double buffering. [packages/react-reconciler/src/ReactFiber.js:327-383]() ### The `alternate` Pointer Each fiber has an `alternate` field pointing to its counterpart in the other tree. [packages/react-reconciler/src/ReactFiber.js:177-177]() ```mermaid graph LR subgraph Current["Current Tree (on screen)"] C_Root["HostRoot"] C_App["App"] end subgraph WorkInProgress["Work-in-Progress Tree (being built)"] W_Root["HostRoot"] W_App["App"] end C_Root <-.alternate.-> W_Root C_App <-.alternate.-> W_App ``` ### Creating Work-in-Progress Fibers The `createWorkInProgress` function manages fiber reuse. [packages/react-reconciler/src/ReactFiber.js:327-383]() 1. Reuses existing `alternate` fiber if available. [packages/react-reconciler/src/ReactFiber.js:342-342]() 2. Resets flags: `flags = NoFlags`, `subtreeFlags = NoFlags`. [packages/react-reconciler/src/ReactFiber.js:358-359]() 3. Copies `pendingProps` and `type` from the current fiber. [packages/react-reconciler/src/ReactFiber.js:364-374]() Sources: [packages/react-reconciler/src/ReactFiber.js:327-383](), [packages/react-reconciler/src/ReactFiberWorkLoop.js:2268-2826]() ## Fiber Tree Structure ### Tree Relationships via Pointers Fibers form a tree using pointer fields. [packages/react-reconciler/src/ReactFiber.js:153-156]() | Pointer Field | Points To | Purpose | |---------------|-----------|---------| | `return` | Parent fiber | Navigate up the tree [packages/react-reconciler/src/ReactFiber.js:153-153]() | | `child` | First child fiber | Navigate down to children [packages/react-reconciler/src/ReactFiber.js:154-154]() | | `sibling` | Next sibling fiber | Navigate across siblings [packages/react-reconciler/src/ReactFiber.js:155-155]() | **Diagram: Tree Traversal and System Entities** ```mermaid graph TB subgraph ReconcilerLogic["ReactFiberBeginWork.js"] direction TB BW["beginWork()"] CW["completeWork()"] end subgraph FiberTree["Fiber Tree (Memory)"] ParentFiber["Fiber (Parent)"] ChildFiber["Fiber (Child)"] SiblingFiber["Fiber (Sibling)"] ParentFiber -->|"child"| ChildFiber ChildFiber -->|"sibling"| SiblingFiber ChildFiber -->|"return"| ParentFiber SiblingFiber -->|"return"| ParentFiber end BW -->|"reconcileChildFibers()"| ParentFiber CW -->|"appendAllChildren()"| ParentFiber ``` Sources: [packages/react-reconciler/src/ReactFiber.js:153-156](), [packages/react-reconciler/src/ReactFiberBeginWork.js:1-100](), [packages/react-reconciler/src/ReactFiberCompleteWork.js:242-247]() ## Fiber Property Deep Dive ### Effect Flags The `flags` field is a bitfield tracking side-effects. [packages/react-reconciler/src/ReactFiberFlags.js:13-81]() | Flag | Meaning | |------|---------| | `Placement` | Node needs to be inserted into the host tree [packages/react-reconciler/src/ReactFiberFlags.js:90-90]() | | `Update` | Node needs a host update [packages/react-reconciler/src/ReactFiberFlags.js:98-98]() | | `ChildDeletion` | Children need to be removed [packages/react-reconciler/src/ReactFiberFlags.js:98-98]() | | `Passive` | `useEffect` needs to run [packages/react-reconciler/src/ReactFiberFlags.js:103-103]() | Sources: [packages/react-reconciler/src/ReactFiberFlags.js:88-121]() ### Scheduling and Lanes `lanes` and `childLanes` track work priority. [packages/react-reconciler/src/ReactFiberLane.js:180-221]() - `lanes`: Work scheduled on this specific fiber. [packages/react-reconciler/src/ReactFiber.js:174-174]() - `childLanes`: Work scheduled in the subtree. [packages/react-reconciler/src/ReactFiber.js:175-175]() During `completeWork`, child lanes are bubbled up to the parent. [packages/react-reconciler/src/ReactFiberCompleteWork.js:1443-1543]() Sources: [packages/react-reconciler/src/ReactFiberLane.js:180-221](), [packages/react-reconciler/src/ReactFiberCompleteWork.js:1443-1543]() ## Profiler and Performance Fields When `enableProfilerTimer` is active, fibers track timing data. [packages/react-reconciler/src/ReactFiber.js:179-197]() | Field | Purpose | |-------|---------| | `actualDuration` | Time spent rendering this fiber [packages/react-reconciler/src/ReactFiber.js:193-193]() | | `actualStartTime` | When rendering started [packages/react-reconciler/src/ReactFiber.js:194-194]() | | `selfBaseDuration` | Estimated time to render without children [packages/react-reconciler/src/ReactFiber.js:195-195]() | Sources: [packages/react-reconciler/src/ReactFiber.js:179-197](), [packages/react-reconciler/src/ReactProfilerTimer.js:123-141]() --- # Page: Work Loop and Rendering Phases # Work Loop and Rendering Phases
